raw_html:
Build What’s Next with Us 

At SEAhub Asia, your career isn't just a job — it's a launchpad.
We move fast, think big, and build futures together.

What’s waiting for you here?

⚡ Remote-first roles built for freedom and flexibility
 🚀 Learning & Development that sharpens skills and accelerates careers
 💰 Competitive salary packages for high performers
 🩺 Wellbeing perks that help you thrive inside and outside work
 🤝 A team that supports, celebrates, and wins as one


 …and we’re just getting started.

We’re growing across Southeast Asia — and we’re searching for sharp minds, big thinkers and curious hearts ready to help us build what’s next.  learners, and big thinkers who want to create what’s next.

If you're ready to make a real impact and grow with a future-ready team, your next chapter starts here.


Job Summary

Support the Data Analytics team by assisting with data validation, report generation, and basic analysis to help ensure data accuracy and enable informed business decision-making. 


Responsibilities:

  1. Reporting, Analysis & Insight Communication

    1. Prepare reports and perform data analysis to support data-driven decision-making

    2. Monitor key business performance metrics to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies

    3. Assist in building and maintaining dashboards and reports for performance tracking

    4. Communicate data insights to stakeholders in a clear, concise, and actionable way

  2. Data Quality & Validation

    1. Support the validation and upkeep of data accuracy across dashboards and data pipelines

  3. Cross-Team Collaboration

    1. Respond to data requests from cross-functional teams, including coordinating requirements and providing basic analytical support

  4. Process Improvement & Documentation

    1. Document data processes and workflows, and contribute to improving existing data practices and standards

    2. Explore and apply new tools and methods to enhance the efficiency and quality of data work

  5. Ad-Hoc Analytical Projects

    1. Assist with ad-hoc data tasks and analytical projects as needed




Requirements:

  • 0–1 year of experience in data analysis or a related field.

  • Foundational knowledge of SQL, Excel, and data visualization platforms such as Power BI or Tableau.

  • Exposure to Python for data analysis is an added advantage.

  • Highly detail-oriented with a strong focus on maintaining data quality and accuracy.

  • Strong communication skills with the ability to present findings and insights clearly.

  • Demonstrates a proactive mindset and willingness to learn new tools, technologies, and analytical techniques.

  • Well-organized, with the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks effectively.

job_title: AI Data Scientist, AI/ML CoE, Group Data & Analytics, GSI
job_description: Role Responsibilities:Utilize advanced data analytics techniques and statistical methods to extract insights, patterns, and actionable knowledge from large and complex datasets. Develop machine learning models and algorithms for various use cases including predictive analytics, forecasting, optimization.Work with historical data and real time inputs to identify trends and patterns. Research, experiment, design, develop and implement generative AI models and applications, including prompt engineering, that can autonomously generate content such as images, text, audio &etc. Stay abreast of emerging trends, technologies and methodologies in the fields of data analytics, AI and machine learning. Proactively explore and experiment with new tools and techniques to drive innovation and competitive advantage.Role Requirements:Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, statistics, mathematics or related field.Experience in data analytics, machine learning and AI with a track record of delivering impactful solutions preferably in the banking or financial services industry. Strong understanding of statistical methods and machine learning algorithms. Experience in developing and deploying machine learning models in a production environment.Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and vice versa. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail, with a passion for innovation and continuous learning. Work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment, managing multiple priorities and deadlines effectively.
raw_html:
Role Responsibilities:

  • Utilize advanced data analytics techniques and statistical methods to extract insights, patterns, and actionable knowledge from large and complex datasets. 

  • Develop machine learning models and algorithms for various use cases including predictive analytics, forecasting, optimization.

  • Work with historical data and real time inputs to identify trends and patterns. 

  • Research, experiment, design, develop and implement generative AI models and applications, including prompt engineering, that can autonomously generate content such as images, text, audio &etc. 

  • Stay abreast of emerging trends, technologies and methodologies in the fields of data analytics, AI and machine learning. 

  • Proactively explore and experiment with new tools and techniques to drive innovation and competitive advantage.


Role Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, statistics, mathematics or related field.

  • Experience in data analytics, machine learning and AI with a track record of delivering impactful solutions preferably in the banking or financial services industry. 

  • Strong understanding of statistical methods and machine learning algorithms. 

  • Experience in developing and deploying machine learning models in a production environment.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and vice versa. 

  • Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail, with a passion for innovation and continuous learning. 

  • Work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment, managing multiple priorities and deadlines effectively.

job_description: We are looking for a Data Engineer / Analyst to build and maintain reliable data systems that support reporting, analytics, and AI/ML applications. The role involves developing data pipelines, managing data warehouses, and ensuring high‑quality, accessible data for business and technical teams.Key ResponsibilitiesDevelop and maintain BI reports and dashboards to support business needs.Design and implement ETL/ELT pipelines for efficient data integration.Build and optimize data warehouse and lakehouse architectures.Ensure data quality, reliability, and timely availability of critical datasets.Support analytics and AI/ML workflows, including feature engineering and model pipelines.Collaborate with analysts, data scientists, and engineers to deliver end‑to‑end data solutions.RequirementsDegree in Computer Science or related field.1–3 years of experience in data engineering, analytics, or related roles.Strong SQL skills; proficiency in Python, Java, or Scala preferred.Experience with BI tools (Tableau, PowerBI, Looker).Hands‑on experience with big data frameworks (e.g., Spark, Hive).Exposure to AI/ML data workflows is a plus.Strong command of both English and Chinese is required for daily collaboration with the headquarters in China.Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.Four (4) positions available.
raw_html:
We are looking for a Data Engineer / Analyst to build and maintain reliable data systems that support reporting, analytics, and AI/ML applications. The role involves developing data pipelines, managing data warehouses, and ensuring high‑quality, accessible data for business and technical teams.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and maintain BI reports and dashboards to support business needs.

  • Design and implement ETL/ELT pipelines for efficient data integration.

  • Build and optimize data warehouse and lakehouse architectures.

  • Ensure data quality, reliability, and timely availability of critical datasets.

  • Support analytics and AI/ML workflows, including feature engineering and model pipelines.

  • Collaborate with analysts, data scientists, and engineers to deliver end‑to‑end data solutions.

Requirements

  • Degree in Computer Science or related field.

  • 1–3 years of experience in data engineering, analytics, or related roles.

  • Strong SQL skills; proficiency in Python, Java, or Scala preferred.

  • Experience with BI tools (Tableau, PowerBI, Looker).

  • Hands‑on experience with big data frameworks (e.g., Spark, Hive).

  • Exposure to AI/ML data workflows is a plus.

  • Strong command of both English and Chinese is required for daily collaboration with the headquarters in China.

  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.

  • Four (4) positions available.

raw_html:
About the Role

We are looking for a hands on Senior Data Analyst Engineer to drive data driven decision making across the business. The role focuses on data engineering, business intelligence and analytics, covering the full workflow from data extraction, transformation and integration (ETL) to dashboarding and analysis.

Job Responsibility:

  • Design and manage data pipelines (ETL) across various system (POS, CRM,ERP).

  • Perform data extraction, cleaning and transformation to support analytics.

  • Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and KPI reports.

  • Conduct data analysis to generate actionable insights for business decisions.

  • Implement data models and structured datasets for reporting and analysis.

  • Support data integration and automation processes to improve efficiency.

  • Develop and maintain Python scripts for data processing, data transformation, automation and web scraping activities.

  • Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver solutions.

  • Present findings in a clear and structured manner to support decision making.

Job Requirements:

  • At least 3 years experience in data analytics, data engineering or related roles.

  • Strong hands on experience in Power BI including dashboard development, data modelling and reporting

  • Solid technical skills in SQL,DAX and Power Query with the ability to perform data extraction transformation and validation.

  • Experience in building and managing ETL processes and data integration workflows, ideally across multiple data sources (eg.POS,CRM,ERP).

  • Working knowledge of Python for data processing, automation and scripting (eg.data, transformation, scheduling or workflow optimisation).

  • Advanced Excel skills including data manipulation, validation and analysis.

  • Ability to manage end to end data workflows from data preparation (ETL) to dashboard delivery and insights generation.

  • Good communication skills with experience working with business stakeholders to understand requirements and present finding clearly.

Added Advantage :

  • Experience with F&B or retail data environments

  • Background in finance, accounting or financial data analysis.

  • Experience cto POS, CRM,ERP systems

  • Experience working with regional or multi country datasets

raw_html:
We are looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst to transform complex data into meaningful insights that support business decisions, improve performance, and identify growth opportunities.

The ideal candidate should have strong analytical skills, hands-on technical experience, and the ability to work closely with stakeholders across different departments. This role will involve dashboard development, data analysis, business reporting, and leading analytics initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

  • Analyse large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, risks, opportunities, and actionable insights.

  • Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards, reports, and analytical models.

  • Work closely with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into data-driven solutions.

  • Define, monitor, and report key performance indicators to support business objectives.

  • Ensure data accuracy, integrity, consistency, and compliance with data governance standards.

  • Lead end-to-end analytics projects, from requirement gathering to analysis, visualisation, reporting, and deployment.

  • Present data findings and recommendations clearly to management and business teams.

  • Support the implementation of advanced analytics initiatives and data-driven strategies.

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams including IT, operations, finance, sales, marketing, and management.

  • Mentor junior analysts and provide technical guidance when required.

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Business Analytics, or a related field.

  •   5+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, reporting, or a related role.

  • Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, and analysis.

  • Experience with Python, R, or similar analytical programming languages.

  • Hands-on experience with data visualisation tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Grafana, or similar platforms.

  • Good understanding of data warehousing, data modelling, and ETL processes.

  • Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud Platform will be an added advantage.

  • Proven ability to analyse large datasets and convert findings into actionable business insights.

  • Strong communication and presentation skills.

  • Good stakeholder management and problem-solving skills.

  • Experience leading analytics projects or mentoring junior analysts is preferred.

raw_html:
Key Accountability Areas

  • Analyse monthly financial and operational performance and highlight key gaps, risks, and insights.

  • Assess profitability across channels, products, and segments, including performance drivers and variances.

  • Evaluate project and investment viability using IRR, NPV, and payback period.

  • Enhance dashboards, trackers, and reporting tools to strengthen performance visibility.

  • Monitor initiatives against targets, forecasts, and action plans.

Performance Reporting

  • Review MTD, QTD, and YTD scorecards against budget and SPLY across volume, margin, OPEX, PBT, and CAPEX.

  • Benchmark and update KPIs and targets to align with business priorities.

Management and Board Reporting

  • Review reporting packs and TM1 submissions to ensure alignment, accuracy, and variance analysis.

  • Prepare the Regional Business Report in RM and USD within timeline.

  • Prepare monthly LT packs and quarterly Board papers, including the annual Plan and Budget presentation.

  • Coordinate timely inputs from relevant functions across the Company.

Planning and Budget Control

  • Coordinate the Company’s five-year plan and budget across volume, margin, OPEX, and other income.

  • Develop OPEX guidelines, consolidate divisional budgets, and align submissions with approved strategies.

  • Review Business as Usual and Growth costs to support management and Board decisions.

  • Align submissions across reporting formats.

Budget Governance and Ad-hoc Support

  • Manage budget variances and supplementary or transfer requests for OPEX and CAPEX.

  • Validate supporting documents, advise on LOA, and ensure compliance with procedures.

  • Track utilisation trends and prepare YEP figures for management submission.

  • Support ad-hoc financial accounting tasks, improvement initiatives, and policy implementation.

Job Requirements:

  • Degree in Accounting or its equivalent qualifications.

  • At least 3 years working experience with good knowledge / experience in research, business planning, strategy development and Microsoft office application and PowerBI.

  • Experience in SAP system is an advantage

  • Possess an overview of the company’s business and operations and the petroleum industry.

  • Strong analytical, communication (written and verbal) presentation and interpersonal skills.

  • Good knowledge / experience in research, business planning, strategy development and Microsoft office application

raw_html:
Company description:

Alliance Bank Malaysia Berhad



Job description:

Key Responsibilities

Financial Performance

  • Prepare GBTB division monthly business performance reports for submission to Group Finance & senior management

  • Prepare monthly sales production and financial forecast with analysis to highlight risks and opportunities

  • Conduct weekly / monthly review with business sales teams to update on business performance against financial / KPI targets, provide variance analysis and highlight opportunities & risk areas

Business Planning

  • Assist Group Chief Business & Transaction Banking Officer on developing business plans, implement strategic initiatives, prepare annual budget, KPI and scorecards

Expense Management

  • Manage expense reporting and accruals, monitor operating expenses and capital expenditure against budget

  • Conduct periodic cost allocation and service level agreements review for GBTB

Financial Modelling

  • Perform financial simulations / modelling to generate profitability analysis for potential new investments (e.g. SME Expansion, Digital SME & etc.)


Job Requirements

  • A recognize degree in preferably Accounting/ Finance/ Mathematics/ Statistics

  • Minimum 6 years of working experience in similar field

  • Strong analytical skills

  • Independent, resourceful and meticulous in work approach with excellent interpersonal skills

raw_html:
Why join us?

Some careers have more impact than others. If you’re looking for a career where you can make a real impression, join HSBC and discover how valued you’ll be. HSBC is one of the largest banking and financial services organisations in the world, with operations in 64 countries and territories. We aim to be where the growth is, enabling businesses to thrive and economies to prosper, and, ultimately, helping people to fulfil their hopes and realise their ambitions.

We are seeking individuals to join HSBC'S Wholesale department in the role of Credit Analysis Unit.


The Opportunity

What you’ll do:

  • The Role requires the Analyst to be a part Corporates/FIG Credit Analysis Team in Kuala Lumpur which supports APAC. In addition, the Analyst would also be required to liaise with the business and locally with the supporting Departments which would enable him/her to carry out the duties.


Role Responsibilities: 

  • Undertake high quality industry, competitor, ESG and client level financial analysis for Global Banking (GB) and Commercial Banking (CMB) Relationship Management (RM) Team.

  • Analysis of financial documentation and supplemental data to support lending decision-making and recommending credit requests to appropriately meet business needs.

  • Preparing credit analyses in support of new and existing business relationships.

  • Support the RM team in the continuous collection, capture and analysis of key data and information related to GB/CMB client’s emissions-intensive activities, climate commitments and low-carbon business initiatives and transition and physical risks to help build a thorough understanding of each client’s plans to transition to net zero emissions over time.

  • Personally develop and maintain strong working relationships with GB & CMB Relationship Managers / Associates along with executives in Risk to ensure that all risks to the business, financial and non-financial, are properly recognized and thoughtfully mitigated in proposals.

  • Ensure that all work is delivered within agreed timeframes, PLAs and meets the required high-quality standard.

  • Understand and comply with all relevant policies, FIM guidelines and procedures issued by the Group.

  • Pro-actively suggest and design improvements in process workflow, where applicable.

  • Communicate clearly with team members and other parts of GBM as required.

  • Maintain HSBC internal control standards.

  • Need to implement the Group Compliance Policy, as applicable to their role.

  • Need to be aware of the Operational Risk scenario associated with their role, and to ensure that all actions during their employment with HSBC take account of the likelihood of operational risk occurring.

  • Adhering strictly to compliance and operational risk controls in accordance with HSBC and regulatory standards, policies and practices; reporting weaknesses, compliance breaches and operational loss events.

  • However, the final position title may vary depending on the candidate's relevant experience, skill and competence. Additionally, they may be assigned additional responsibilities such as conducting quality checks, mentoring and training new joiners.


What you will need to succeed in the role:

  • C.A. / CFA / MBA / Bachelor Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting or equivalent is essential, and any professional qualification is desirable.

  • Professional Credit Certification from AICB, sustainability knowledge is an advantage.

  • 2 to 6 years of relevant experience in Credit Analysis.

  • Sustainability knowledge is an advantage

  • Highly proficient at Excel and Word.

  • Excellent verbal/written communication skills in English, proficient in credit writing in English, hard-working, organized, diligent, and above all a good team player.

  • Cantonese and/or Mandarin language capabilities are highly preferred. The analyst should be able to speak and read financial statements in Cantonese/Mandarin language.

  • Good command of credit analysis and financial knowledge.


What additional skills will be good to have?

  • The role holder is expected to have subject matter expertise of end-to-end credit processes & corporate banking products.

company: Whale Cloud Technology Malaysia Sdn Bhd
job_description: Pre-sales technical support, including requirement analysis, formulation of pre-sales technical solutions, bid preparation, response to inquiries, pre-sales project proof of concept (POC), assessment of feasibility workload, pre-sales customer presentations, and solution consultations. Requirement research involves repeatedly communicating with and clarifying customer needs, and writing Software Requirements Specifications (SRS). Based on project progress requirements and needs, and considering research and development capacity, develop a reasonable version output plan:3.1) Communicate with customers, project managers, and internal R&D personnel to meet customer requirements.3.2) Develop a reasonable project plan for the R&D department to ensure compliance with project time and manpower requirements. Track the version delivery process:1) Requirement review; 2) Requirement import; 3) Requirement tracking. Assist sales in completing secondary sales tasks:5.1) Organize communication meetings with customers, operators, project managers, and project-related personnel.5.2) Proactively identify project risk points based on product and operator operational conditions and propose reasonable and feasible improvement solutions.5.3) Actively explore customer needs and create opportunities for secondary sales contracts. Provide daily technical support for post-sales projects.Job Requirements Full-time bachelor's degree or above, including but not limited to majors such as Telecommunications, Electronics, Computer Science, Software, Marketing, etc.  Experience in market analysis, pre-sales solutions, testing, and delivery work. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.  Preferred background in industries such as Software, Finance etc. Willingness to travel internationally as needed. Capable of engaging in effective communication with clients, understanding their needs correctly, clearly, and comprehensively, designing consulting solutions based on client requirements, and conveying design ideas and work results clearly to clients. Proficient in understanding, communication, and expression, with strong logical thinking and communication skills, Fluent in English and Chinese language to effectively communicate with China counterparts.
raw_html:

raw_html:
The vacancy

AIS are looking for a FP&A Data Analyst who can take ownership of monthly forecasting processes, ensuring data is accurate, consistent, and ready for senior leadership review. The role focuses on using advanced Excel and automation tools to streamline reporting, improve efficiency, and drive continuous improvements, while confidently engaging with stakeholders across the business to challenge data and support decision-making.

Key responsibilities include:

  • Manage the consolidation of monthly forecasts received from Operations, Sales, and subsidiary entities (primarily Excel-based inputs)

  • Perform initial analysis and validation of submitted forecasts, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and consistency ahead of Finance Senior Leadership review

  • Develop a strong understanding of the end-to-end forecasting process, including data sources, assumptions, and drivers across different business units

  • Identify and deliver automation opportunities to streamline the consolidation and reporting process, working closely with Operations, Sales, and Finance teams

  • Implement improvements across various finance areas to reduce manual effort, standardise inputs, and enhance the reliability and speed of reporting

  • Maintain and enhance existing automated processes, particularly those built using Power Query and Excel-based solutions

  • Engage with stakeholders to challenge and validate variances, ensuring explanations are clearly understood and supported before escalation to senior stakeholders

  • Ensure data accuracy and integrity within TM1, supporting the maintenance and reliability of financial data

  • Play a key role in implementation of a Group Reporting pack, contributing to the design, standardisation, and rollout across business units

  • Actively contribute to future system and process improvements, including the implementation of a new ERP system, ensuring alignment with reporting and data requirements

  • Drive continuous improvement across Finance

What are we looking for in our FP&A Data Analyst: 

  • Strong Excel skills (advance level, comfortable with large datasets)

  • 3+ years’ experience in data analysis, reporting, finance or business environment.

  • Proven advanced experience with Power Query

  • Proven ability to improve or automate processes

  • Strong stakeholder communication skills

  • Strong interest in using AI tools *Artificial Intelligence (e.g. CoPilot) to improve efficiency and automate tasks

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, with the ability to engage credibly with technical and commercial stakeholders

  • Degree in Accounting, Finance or STEM

  • Professional qualification (ACCA / CIMA / CPA) – qualified or in progress

What are the benefits of being our FP&A Data Analyst: 

  • Up to RM 110,000.00 per annum DOE 

  • Discretionary annual bonus of up to 15% of basic salary

  • Private Medical Insurance

job_title: Analyst/ Senior Analyst, Corporate Banking - Kuala Lumpur
job_description: Key Responsibilities:Understand clients’ financing requirements, perform detailed analysis and evaluation, structure detailed financing terms and ensure credit papers produced are of high quality to achieve high quality of loan growth to ensure capital efficiency and minimize Gross Impaired Loan Ratio (GILR).Prepare proposal and/or detailed terms sheet for pitching and negotiation with clients. Drive end to end approval process including syndication with credit risk and presentation in GMCC / CRC / Board followed by negotiation on loan documentation for release and account monitoring. Identify business opportunities and cross sell GB products as Business Origination.Understand clients’ business plans and strategies forward, and be familiar with clients’ organization structure, key management and decision making process.Accountable for detailed account planning by analysing (part of annual review of the accounts) and evaluating product applications / relevance to client’s business plans/requirements and discuss accordingly with Associate Director.Establish and develop business relationship with existing clients and new clients.Pre-deal engagement with internal stakeholders on lending strategy.Requirements:Bachelor’s Degree in Business Finance, Banking or Accounting, Engineering, Science or Mathematics.Experience in credit analysis or in similar position.Strong analytical skills with good grasp of financial analysis.Excellent interpersonal and writing skills to ensure effective delivery of credit reports and communication with stakeholders.Experience in Business Banking, Commercial Banking, Debt Capital Markets, or experience as a relationship manager or a credit officer.Good knowledge of credit risk assessment and financial analysis.Sound industry or market knowledge.
raw_html:
Key Responsibilities:

  • Understand clients’ financing requirements, perform detailed analysis and evaluation, structure detailed financing terms and ensure credit papers produced are of high quality to achieve high quality of loan growth to ensure capital efficiency and minimize Gross Impaired Loan Ratio (GILR).

  • Prepare proposal and/or detailed terms sheet for pitching and negotiation with clients. Drive end to end approval process including syndication with credit risk and presentation in GMCC / CRC / Board followed by negotiation on loan documentation for release and account monitoring. Identify business opportunities and cross sell GB products as Business Origination.

  • Understand clients’ business plans and strategies forward, and be familiar with clients’ organization structure, key management and decision making process.

  • Accountable for detailed account planning by analysing (part of annual review of the accounts) and evaluating product applications / relevance to client’s business plans/requirements and discuss accordingly with Associate Director.

  • Establish and develop business relationship with existing clients and new clients.

  • Pre-deal engagement with internal stakeholders on lending strategy.


Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Business Finance, Banking or Accounting, Engineering, Science or Mathematics.

  • Experience in credit analysis or in similar position.

  • Strong analytical skills with good grasp of financial analysis.

  • Excellent interpersonal and writing skills to ensure effective delivery of credit reports and communication with stakeholders.

  • Experience in Business Banking, Commercial Banking, Debt Capital Markets, or experience as a relationship manager or a credit officer.

  • Good knowledge of credit risk assessment and financial analysis.

  • Sound industry or market knowledge.

raw_html:
About the role

Keep CoinGecko's commercial engine running by optimising the end-to-end sales process, maintaining data quality, and building automations that give the sales team leverage. You'll own operational dashboards, contribute to forecasting, and work cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, and Finance. Based in Malaysia.

Benefits

  • Transparent, levelled salary with clear career progression

  • Remote-first role based in Malaysia

  • Direct exposure to B2B SaaS sales operations in a high-growth crypto company

  • Opportunity to build and ship automation workflows with real business impact

What will my day look like?

  • Maintain sales process documentation, playbooks, and CRM (HubSpot) data hygiene

  • Build and update operational dashboards; implement AI-assisted insight tools

  • Validate weekly pipeline data and support forecasting accuracy

  • Identify automation opportunities across the sales funnel using Zapier, n8n, or AI tools

  • Support strategic planning with data analysis and commercial operations reporting

Skills & experience

  • 2–3 years in sales operations, business operations, or an analytical role

  • At least 1 year in a customer-facing position

  • Proficient in HubSpot CRM and Excel/Google Sheets

  • Exposure to adjacent tools: Xero, BI systems, B2B data platforms, or marketing tools

  • Experience with workflow automation tools (Zapier, n8n, Google App Script, or AI tools)

About the company

CoinGecko is a global leader in cryptocurrency data, operating since 2014. We track 10,000+ tokens across 400+ exchanges, serve 300M+ page views in 100+ countries, and handle 45B+ API calls monthly. We believe blockchain and cryptocurrency will define the future of finance — and we're building the data infrastructure to serve billions.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Economics, Finance, Property Development, Real Estate, Urban Planning, Marketing, Statistics, Data Analytics, or a related field.

  • Minimum 2 to 5 years of relevant working experience in research, market analysis, business intelligence, property development, consultancy, or related fields.

  • Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to conduct quantitative and qualitative research and interpret findings effectively.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, PowerPoint, and Word.

  • Experience in preparing reports, presentations, feasibility studies, and market assessments.

  • A good understanding of the Malaysian property market, economic trends, and business environment will be an added advantage.

  • Familiarity with data visualization tools, statistical software, GIS, Power BI, or similar analytical tools will be an added advantage.

  • Strong communication, report writing, and presentation skills.

  • Able to work independently and manage multiple assignments within stipulated timelines.

Responsibilities

  • Conduct market research, industry analysis, competitor studies, and business intelligence gathering to support the Company's strategic planning and product development initiatives.

  • Monitor and analyze economic indicators, property market trends, consumer behavior, demographic data, and industry developments relevant to the Company's business activities.

  • Collect, compile, verify, and analyze data from various sources, including government publications, industry reports, consultants, market surveys, and internal databases.

  • Prepare research reports, market assessments, feasibility studies, presentations, and briefing papers for management's review and decision-making.

  • Conduct competitor benchmarking and evaluate market positioning, product offerings, pricing strategies, and emerging industry trends.

  • Assist in identifying new business opportunities, investment prospects, land acquisition opportunities, and potential market segments.

  • Support product planning and development by providing research-based insights and recommendations.

  • Prepare demand analysis, catchment studies, customer profiling, and market penetration assessments where required.

  • Maintain and update research databases, market intelligence records, and reference materials.

  • Coordinate with consultants, government agencies, industry bodies, and external stakeholders to obtain relevant information and data.

  • Assist in preparing presentations, proposals, and management papers for internal discussions and approval processes.

  • Monitor regulatory changes, policies, guidelines, and legislation that may impact the Company's operations, projects, and business strategies.

  • Present research findings and recommendations to management and project teams when required.

  • Ensure confidentiality and proper management of sensitive business information and research materials.

  • Perform any other duties and responsibilities as assigned by management.

Role Responsibilities:

  • Lead – data foundation – Risk & Finance domain: act as a data domain specialist for the sectors Risk & Finance, leading efforts on addressing data pain-points and enhancing the foundation.

  • Analytics: Develop predictive risk models e.g. collections scoring and segmentation using analytics to create improved, risk-differentiated collection strategies, leading to ECL savings and/or operational cost reduction. Support analytics enhancements in Finance, working with the sector.

  • Dash-boarding: Champion sectoral priorities of Business Intelligence (BI) and develop effective dash board to elevate data democratization, decision management and MIS / reporting.

  • Evaluation and Adoption of Advanced Tools: Identify and evaluate cutting-edge tools and artificial intelligence solutions to enhance the Group's capabilities in leveraging data for management.


Role Requirements:

  • Minimum of 10 years of experience in risk management, finance, analytics, or a related field within the financial services industry. Proven experience in data & analytics.

  • Excellent analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and draw actionable insights.

  • Ability to design and implement effective monitoring and management processes.

  • Experience with data programming languages and familiarity with credit modelling.

  • Familiarity with BI dash-boarding and reporting tools.

  • Preferred exposure in machine learning, and AI applications in risk management.

  • Good communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

  • In-depth understanding of the financial services industry, particularly in relation to risk / finance / allied domains.

  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to maintaining high-quality standards.

  • Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and manage multiple priorities.

raw_html:
ABOUT MISTER MOBILE

With 14 years in the industry, Mister Mobile is Singapore's trusted leader in mobile services. We empower people through technology and deliver outstanding customer experiences — from the best trade-in prices to rigorously tested devices and warm, personalised service.

We're proud to hold ISO certification, CaseTrust accreditation, and a Carousell InstantBuy Award, backed by over 15,000+ positive reviews and counting.

 At Mister Mobile, we're not just focused on technology — we're focused on people. We foster a collaborative, inclusive workplace where growth, innovation, and teamwork are at the heart of everything we do. 


THE ROLE

We're looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Project/Junior BA Executive to support the coordination and execution of internal projects across departments. This role is well-suited for someone with experience in administrative operations or project support, especially in fast-paced environments.

You'll track timelines, follow up on tasks, coordinate across teams, and keep project activities moving smoothly. The ideal candidate is organised, proactive, and able to work independently while keeping stakeholders aligned and informed.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

Project Coordination & Follow-Up

  • Coordinate and follow up on project timelines, tasks, and dependencies across departments.

  • Track daily progress, flag delays or risks early, and ensure projects move forward without stalling.

Task Tracking & Workflow Support

  • Set up, maintain, and update project boards and task trackers in ClickUp.

  • Apply simple, practical Agile workflows — Kanban boards, checklists — to improve visibility, ownership, and accountability.

Communication & Meeting Support

  • Take meeting notes and prepare clear task summaries after every session.

  • Communicate decisions, action items, and next steps to all relevant stakeholders in a timely, structured manner.

Administrative & Coordination Support

  • Handle scheduling, file updates, and shared folder management.

  • Ensure project information is organized, up to date, and easily accessible at all times.

 Process Improvement

  • Spot inefficiencies or gaps during day-to-day execution.

  • Suggest practical improvements to help teams work more effectively.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

  • Bachelor's Degree in Business, Computer Science, or a related field.

  • 1–3 years of experience in project coordination, administrative support, or operations.

  • Hands-on experience with project management tools such as ClickUp, Notion, or similar platforms.

  • Comfortable with Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and Google Workspace.

  • Well-organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple timelines independently.

  • Strong communication, coordination, and problem-solving skills.

  • Able to communicate clearly with internal teams and external stakeholders.

job_description: JOB PURPOSEManage the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) framework of the Group and work with the business to consolidate and analyze the development and implementation of ESG strategy to enhance the Group’s sustainability performance.DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIESThe position requires the employee to perform the following:1.    Policies and strategies: Compile and research to produce data analytics and reports that include ESG related factors, risks and opportunities to support and achieve the department’s objectives, which are in line with the company’s vision and mission. 2.    Communication materials – ESG: Gather and analyse ESG related data and information, conduct materiality survey to support the timely production of draft sustainability reports. (i.e. ESG reports, sustainability reports and annual/integrated reports, exco proposals, ESG surveys, etc.) 3.    Brand communication: Analyse and report any pertinent ESG issues that could impact the company’s brand reputation for effective brand communication. (i.e. including peer analysis and sustainability benchmarks using ESG measures, standards and methodologies to keep informed of the latest developments in the industry) 4.    Media communication: Compile content on ESG to prepare media related drafts to ensure delivery of accurate and timely information. 5.    Projects: Browse and report any pertinent ESG issues that could impact the department’s Projects for effective brand communication. Prepare draft proposals for review. 6.    Budgeting & Administration: Monitor any costs incurred in relation to ESG to ensure the costs are within department’s budget and provide value-added inputs to ensure cost efficiency of the implementation of the ESG works. 7.    Procurement: Liaise and prepare the vendor selection process (comparative analysis and recommendation) to select vendors/agencies on ESG works (if any) to provide timely information for decision making. Administer payment of invoices (if any) to provide timely payment of completed work to vendors. 8.    Corporate website: Administer the sustainability/ESG section of the company’s corporate website to ensure the ESG content is updated, accurate and consistent (where applicable) across the Company and its subsidiaries. 9.    Content archives: Collage and archive all ESG files and past works (including final and working files, references, research materials and multimedia materials) with a proper documentation and filing system for easy retrieval. 10.  Event management: Administer the assigned ESG-related events (such as ESG reporting data collation briefings to company departments and subsidiaries to develop measurable sustainability initiatives for external reporting) to ensure smooth running of the event. 11.  Management papers: Gather and provide ESG findings to support the drafting of management papers/ presentation slides to provide accuracy of information and timely completion of task. 12.  Perform any other duties and responsibilities as and when assigned by the Superior to ensure timely completion and meet the department’s objectives. KNOWLEDGE / EXPERIENCEMinimum working experience: 3-5 yearsMinimum qualification: Bachelor’s DegreeArea / Field of Study: ESG Data Analyst / Sustainability / Business Finance / Mathematics / CommunicationSpecific skill / knowledge required:Experiences: 1-3 years of sustainability reporting or data analytics reporting experienceSkills: Good analytical skills (including logical reasoning, critical thinking, communication, research and data analysis), creativity and writing skills.Knowledge: Good understanding of the history and corporate working culture of the Company and the Group (subsidiaries)
raw_html:
JOB PURPOSE

Manage the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) framework of the Group and work with the business to consolidate and analyze the development and implementation of ESG strategy to enhance the Group’s sustainability performance.


DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

The position requires the employee to perform the following:

1.    Policies and strategies: Compile and research to produce data analytics and reports that include ESG related factors, risks and opportunities to support and achieve the department’s objectives, which are in line with the company’s vision and mission.

 

2.    Communication materials – ESG: Gather and analyse ESG related data and information, conduct materiality survey to support the timely production of draft sustainability reports. (i.e. ESG reports, sustainability reports and annual/integrated reports, exco proposals, ESG surveys, etc.)

 

3.    Brand communication: Analyse and report any pertinent ESG issues that could impact the company’s brand reputation for effective brand communication. (i.e. including peer analysis and sustainability benchmarks using ESG measures, standards and methodologies to keep informed of the latest developments in the industry)

 

4.    Media communication: Compile content on ESG to prepare media related drafts to ensure delivery of accurate and timely information.

 

5.    Projects: Browse and report any pertinent ESG issues that could impact the department’s Projects for effective brand communication. Prepare draft proposals for review.

 

6.    Budgeting & Administration: Monitor any costs incurred in relation to ESG to ensure the costs are within department’s budget and provide value-added inputs to ensure cost efficiency of the implementation of the ESG works.

 

7.    Procurement: Liaise and prepare the vendor selection process (comparative analysis and recommendation) to select vendors/agencies on ESG works (if any) to provide timely information for decision making. Administer payment of invoices (if any) to provide timely payment of completed work to vendors.

 

8.    Corporate website: Administer the sustainability/ESG section of the company’s corporate website to ensure the ESG content is updated, accurate and consistent (where applicable) across the Company and its subsidiaries.

 

9.    Content archives: Collage and archive all ESG files and past works (including final and working files, references, research materials and multimedia materials) with a proper documentation and filing system for easy retrieval.

 

10.  Event management: Administer the assigned ESG-related events (such as ESG reporting data collation briefings to company departments and subsidiaries to develop measurable sustainability initiatives for external reporting) to ensure smooth running of the event.

 

11.  Management papers: Gather and provide ESG findings to support the drafting of management papers/ presentation slides to provide accuracy of information and timely completion of task.

 

12.  Perform any other duties and responsibilities as and when assigned by the Superior to ensure timely completion and meet the department’s objectives.

 

KNOWLEDGE / EXPERIENCE

Minimum working experience: 3-5 years

Minimum qualification: Bachelor’s Degree

Area / Field of Study: ESG Data Analyst / Sustainability / Business Finance / Mathematics / Communication

Specific skill / knowledge required:

Experiences: 1-3 years of sustainability reporting or data analytics reporting experience

Skills: Good analytical skills (including logical reasoning, critical thinking, communication, research and data analysis), creativity and writing skills.

Knowledge: Good understanding of the history and corporate working culture of the Company and the Group (subsidiaries)

At SMITH ZANDER, we pride ourselves in our core values: we focus on premium quality work; we understand our clients well; and we practice an open, respectful culture.

As a performance-based employer, we place a strong emphasis on recognising and rewarding achievements and results. We empower our employees by fostering an environment that encourages independent thinking, ownership of responsibilities, and strategic decision-making.

We are committed to developing, nurturing and retaining our best talents as we aspire to strengthen our position as the leading player in our specialised, niche industry. You will be supported by high performing and dynamic team members who embrace strong team work and team communication. On top of our growth development library, on-the-job trainings and project/task-based feedback, we conduct structured workshop and trainings at least twice a year to equip you with the necessary knowledge, tools, and tricks to excel in your role.

We offer a range of attractive benefits to ensure our team is taken care of. Our monetary benefits include transport allowance, mobile allowance, wellness allowance, dental and eye care, and medical benefits. On top of annual, medical, maternity, paternity and compassionate leaves, we offer additional leaves for festive celebration and birthday. We invest in quality team bonding time through team outings, travels and team building activities.

If you are interested in doing challenging analytical work in a fast-paced corporate environment, yet be immersed in an easy-going and fun office culture with young, dynamic colleagues, we would love to hear from you.


We are looking for driven individuals with the following critical capabilities to join our team:

  • Detailed-oriented and meticulous

  • Strong in articulating your thoughts and ideas

  • Excellent in narrative report-writing in English

  • Resourceful and persistent in data gathering

  • Able to structure and solve analytical problems

  • Comfortable in qualitative and quantitative analysis

  • Versatile in adapting to multi-country, multi-industry projects and have strong business acumen

  • Able to multi-task effectively and work within tight deadlines

  • Preferably 2 year experience in market research or other working experience. Fresh graduates may be considered for entry level position

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in any discipline


The key responsibilities for this role are:

  • Write detailed, concise and professional research reports and business plans, with compelling visualisation

  • Conduct primary and secondary research in various industries

  • Gather, clean, collate and tabulate data systematically and accurately

  • Perform meaningful data analysis (qualitative and quantitative) including developing assumptions and parameters

  • Develop strategic recommendations and quality solutions for clients

  • Maintain ownership on assigned projects

  • Build and foster lasting client relationships


Visit our website for more information on the projects we are involved in (smith-zander.com)

raw_html:
What We’re Looking For:

Join Meltwater's Client Insights team, where you'll step into the role of an Insights Analyst, contributing to our bespoke reports and project management for esteemed clients. Your analytical skills will shape customized reports and assist in managing key projects, utilizing both quantitative and qualitative media analysis to offer actionable insights. Working closely with internal teams and clients, you'll ensure timely delivery of high-quality reports, continuously refining techniques to meet evolving client needs. As a pivotal member of our data-driven approach, you'll collect, organize, and analyze customer research metrics to inform critical business decisions. Your duties will encompass data compilation, analysis, insight generation, and assisting in report development methodologies.

At Meltwater, you'll immerse yourself in an environment fostering continuous growth, where mentorship and inclusive leadership are fundamental principles rather than mere catchphrases. Collaborate with experienced colleagues and respected leaders, continually evolving alongside industry trends. Within this dynamic environment, you'll thrive within a culture emphasizing empowerment and autonomy, making significant contributions to our expanding organization's success.

 

What You'll Do:

This is a remote work opportunity.

  • Lead on newsletter and report production for clients using the Meltwater platform. This can involve the quantitative and qualitative analysis of news and social media data through traditional media research methodologies and generative AI tools to provide clients with actionable insights on brand perception, media performance, and industry trends

  • Grow the team’s expertise in Government and/or PR/marketing insights research, specifically for the Southeast Asian market

  • Independently generate newsletter or report templates, build queries, and process data where needed (e.g. determine relevance, translate articles, visualise data, etc.)

  • Maintain high standards of quality in accuracy, formatting and visual consistency, as well as timeliness of delivery

  • Troubleshoot and address client or internal stakeholder queries and concerns

  • Assist, or in some instances, lead in project management. This can include tracking delivery deadlines, managing vendors and ensuring the data hygiene of internal metrics

  • Contribute towards team initiatives (e.g. workflow optimisation, project renewal)

  • All other relevant tasks as assigned 


Who You Are:

  • 1-3 years of relevant work experience. Experience in Southeast Asian government or Southeast Asian media or consumer insights research would be highly advantageous. Fresh graduates with strong academic and internship profiles with keen interest in media insights may apply

  • Excellent written and spoken English. This role requires you to communicate with English-speaking clients and colleagues frequently, and produce deliverables in English

  • Able to read and translate Chinese to English at a high level, with the assistance of machine translation tools

  • Able to work early hours (5am - 2pm) regularly for newsletter production. Able to flexibly support ad-hoc client deliverables on public holidays and weekends when required (e.g. crisis or campaign reporting). Overtime pay is provided

  • Effectively work in geographically distributed remote teams, combining self-motivation with clear, consistent communication

  • Proficient in Microsoft and Google Suite including PivotTable, VLOOKUP, and similar functions. Knowledge of Python, Google Apps Script and R is a plus

  • Comfortable working with generative AI tools to derive trends and actionable insights

  • Demonstrated ability in critical thinking and problem-solving

  • Demonstrated leadership potential

  • Thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, adapting quickly to new challenges.


What We Offer: 

  • Enjoy flexible paid time off for enhanced work-life balance.

  • Comprehensive health insurance tailored for you, complete with an annual health check.

  • Special discount on selected restaurants/shops through our food delivery partner.

  • Employee assistance programs covering mental health, legal, financial, wellness, and behaviour areas to ensure your overall well-being.

  • Complimentary CalmApp subscription for you and your loved ones, because mental wellness matters.

  • Energetic work environment with a hybrid work style, providing the balance you need.

  • Benefit from our family leave program, which grows with your tenure at Meltwater.

  • Thrive within our inclusive community and seize ongoing professional development opportunities to elevate your career.


Our Story

At Meltwater, we believe that when you have the right people in the right environment, great things happen.


Our best-in-class technology empowers our 27,000 customers around
the world to make better business decisions through data. But we can’t do that without our global team of developers, innovators, problem-solvers, and high-performers who embrace challenges and find new solutions for our customers.


Our award-winning global culture drives everything we do and creates
an environment where our employees can make an impact, learn every day, feel a sense of belonging, and celebrate each other’s successes along
the way.


We are innovators at the core who see the potential in people, ideas and technologies. Together, we challenge ourselves to go big, be bold, and build best-in-class solutions for our customers.


We’re proud of our diverse team of 2,200+ employees in 50 locations across  
25 countries around the world. No matter where you are, you’ll work with people who care about your success and get the support you need to unlock new heights in your career.


We are Meltwater. Inspired by innovation, powered by people.

 

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement


Meltwater is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Prohibits Discrimination and Harassment of Any Kind: At Meltwater, we are dedicated to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where every employee feels valued, respected, and empowered. We are committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity and strive to provide a work environment that is free from discrimination and harassment.

All employment decisions at Meltwater are made based on business needs, job requirements, and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by the applicable laws and regulations.

Meltwater does not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind, and we actively promote a culture of respect, fairness, and inclusivity. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities to apply and join us in our mission to drive innovation and make a positive impact in the world.

raw_html:
Join Our Growing Team at Vistage Malaysia and Be Part of a Globally Recognized Multinational Company!


💼 Perks & Bonuses:

  • Hybrid work flexibility that combines working from home and in the office

  • Annual overseas retreats

  • 1-month contractual bonus + performance bonus

  • Travel & parking allowance + work phone

  • Insurance: dental, optical, GPA, GTL, CI, and GHS


📈 The Opportunity

We are looking for a hands-on and business-oriented Business Intelligence & Digital Transformation Analyst to support and improve our internal systems, reporting, automation, and operational processes.

This is not a highly specialized enterprise IT role. Instead, we are looking for someone with broad practical experience across business systems, IT support, applications, reporting, and operational technology, someone comfortable wearing multiple hats and supporting internal stakeholders directly.

You will work closely with the CEO, CTO, and our IT vendors, taking ownership of day-to-day digital operations, reporting initiatives, system coordination, and process improvement projects.

This role offers strong exposure, autonomy, and a potential long-term growth path into a Digital Transformation Lead or Digital Director role.


Key Responsibilities:

Business Intelligence & Reporting

  • Build, maintain, and improve Power BI dashboards and reports

  • Extract, combine, clean, and transform data from multiple sources

  • Create measures, calculations, and reporting models using Power BI and Power Query

  • Support business analysis and operational reporting needs across departments

  • Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and reporting consistency

  • Perform ad hoc analysis and reporting using Power BI and Excel

Digital Transformation & Process Improvement

  • Identify manual workflows and operational inefficiencies for automation or digital improvement

  • Support AI-enabled and workflow automation initiatives

  • Assist with implementation and coordination of digital projects and system enhancements

  • Document business processes, workflows, and SOPs

Systems & Application Support

  • Provide internal support for business applications, reporting tools, and operational systems

  • Support staff with troubleshooting, issue resolution, and system usage guidance

  • Coordinate with external vendors, MSPs, and software providers on support cases, enhancements, and technical issues

  • Assist in maintaining operational continuity and visibility across core business systems

  • Perform initial investigation and escalation when system or data issues arise

CRM / Business Platform Support

  • Support and maintain CRM or business management platforms

  • Assist users with system adoption, reporting, workflows, and operational improvements

  • Work with stakeholders to improve business processes and data quality within systems

Stakeholder Coordination & Project Ownership

  • Bridge communication between business users, vendors, technical teams, and management

  • Manage tasks and priorities independently with strong ownership and follow-through

  • Support project coordination, user acceptance testing (UAT), and implementation activities


Required Skills & Experience:

  • 3–5 years of relevant experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, Business Systems, IT Support, or Digital Transformation roles

  • Strong proficiency in Power BI

  • Strong Excel skills (Pivot Tables, Power Query, Lookup functions & data analysis)

  • Experience supporting internal users across systems, applications, or operational technology

  • Strong troubleshooting and customer support mindset

  • Experience coordinating with external vendors, MSPs, or software providers

  • Comfortable providing both in-person and remote support

  • Strong documentation and communication skills

  • Ability to work independently with strong ownership and accountability

Preferred / Advantageous Experience

  • Experience supporting CRM or ERP platforms such as Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho, or similar systems

  • Exposure to workflow automation or AI-related initiatives

  • Working knowledge of SQL; Python is an advantage

  • Experience supporting websites, mobile apps, or digital product environments

  • Experience working between business users, product managers, developers, and vendors


Kindly click “Quick Apply” if you are ready to join us today!

job_description: NAVI, short for Navigate, Advise, Value and International, is a corporate advisory firm HQ in Singapore with offices in KL and HK. The company aims to build meaningful relationships with clients by providing bespoke services and value-adding advice to help navigate growth paths and achieve objectives. NAVI offers business valuation services for deal advisory and financial reporting.We are recruiting analysts who wish to pursue a career in business valuation profession. The analyst will have exposure to various valuation project such as business & intangible assets valuation, impairment assessment, financial instruments valuation. Additionally, the role will have direct client interaction in managing business valuation assignments and opportunity to travel overseas for site visit.The member will be a part of our KL office located at Bangsar South and will have the opportunity to work on cross-border assignments within the region.

NAVI, short for Navigate, Advise, Value and International, is a corporate advisory firm HQ in Singapore with offices in KL and HK. The company aims to build meaningful relationships with clients by providing bespoke services and value-adding advice to help navigate growth paths and achieve objectives. NAVI offers business valuation services for deal advisory and financial reporting.

We are recruiting analysts who wish to pursue a career in business valuation profession. The analyst will have exposure to various valuation project such as business & intangible assets valuation, impairment assessment, financial instruments valuation. Additionally, the role will have direct client interaction in managing business valuation assignments and opportunity to travel overseas for site visit.

The member will be a part of our KL office located at Bangsar South and will have the opportunity to work on cross-border assignments within the region.

raw_html:
Job Summary 

The Data Administrator is responsible for maintaining the accuracy, integrity, and confidentiality of operational data across company systems. This role supports business operations through data entry, record maintenance, process coordination, reporting, and operational research activities. The position plays an important role in ensuring efficient workflows, supporting decision-making, and identifying opportunities for continuous operational improvement. 

Key Responsibilities 

Data Management & Maintenance 

  • Enter, update, and maintain data in company systems and databases.  

  • Ensure data accuracy, completeness, and consistency across multiple platforms.  

  • Perform regular data audits, validation checks, and cleansing activities.  

  • Identify and resolve data discrepancies, duplicates, and errors.  

Database Administration 

  • Maintain and organize company databases, records, and master data files.  

  • Support database updates, migrations, and data imports/exports.  

  • Monitor database integrity and report issues to relevant stakeholders.  

  • Assist in maintaining data structures and documentation.  

Reporting & Analysis Support 

  • Generate scheduled operational reports and dashboards.  

  • Prepare and compile data for management review and decision-making.  

  • Support Data Analysts by providing clean, validated, and structured datasets.  

  • Assist with basic trend analysis and data reconciliation tasks.  

Data Governance & Compliance 

  • Ensure compliance with company data management policies and procedures.  

  • Maintain confidentiality and security of sensitive information.  

  • Support data retention, archival, and record management requirements.  

  • Assist in developing and maintaining data standards and SOPs.  

Process Improvement 

  • Recommend improvements to data collection and management processes.  

  • Participate in system enhancement and data quality initiatives.  

  • Collaborate with business users to improve data accuracy and workflow efficiency.  

 

Requirements 

Education 

  • Minimum SPM or Diploma or Bachelor's Degree in Information Systems, Business Administration, Computer Science, Statistics, or related field.  

Experience 

  • 1–3 years of experience in data administration, data management, data entry, or related roles is preferred.  

  • Experience working with large datasets and business systems.  

Technical Skills 

  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, Power Query preferred).  

  • Familiarity with database systems and data management tools.  

  • Basic SQL knowledge is an advantage.  

  • Experience with ERP, CRM, or business information systems is preferred.  

  • Familiarity with Power BI, Tableau, or reporting tools is a plus.  

Soft Skills 

  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy.  

  • Excellent organizational and time management skills.  

  • Analytical and problem-solving mindset.  

  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively.  

  • Good communication and documentation skills.

raw_html:
As an FP&A Analyst within the APAC Global Business Services Centre (GBS), you will have the opportunity to play a role in defining and supporting our future as well as being part of a new and exciting team. Partnering with our Finance Leaders and Business CoE’s you will ensure the delivery of insightful management and strategic reporting and be accountable for the production of Business Unit planning, reporting and analysis activities.

In this role, you'll play a key part in finance generalists, working alongside a dynamic team that values innovation, collaboration, and continuous growth. 

Your key responsibilities will include: 

  • Deliver a financial planning process that meets stakeholder requirements, supporting the delivery of ConvaTec's BU operational and strategic goals.

  • Develop insight into potential Corporate, Business Unit and supporting Function performance gaps, enabling transparency on the success of actions taken within the Business and highlighting areas where course correction is required, through the development and provision of balanced scorecard reporting.

  • Work closely with FBPs and Business to ensure that the defined planning, reporting and analysis is delivered in an efficient, controlled manner, delivering insight and value to the business.

  • Deliver the strategic plan process, utilizing inputs from FBPs, Business CoEs and Business stakeholders.

  • Champion a comprehensive framework to guide the planning process and assist the business in developing its strategy.

  • Deliver on the production of the annual budget, working closely with FBPs and business stakeholders.

  • Deliver on the production of financial forecasts.

  • Provide Business Unit specific inputs into the first draft of strategic companywide reporting to senior leadership.

  • Deliver on the production of management reporting, in line with related SLAs.

  • Provide proactive and reactive analytical support to FBPs and the Business as required.

To be successful you will: 

  • FP&A and/or controlling experience, including some financial modelling

  • Previous planning reporting and analysis experience in FP&A, Controlling or Accounting positions (essential) – 2 to 8 years

  • Previous Shared Service Centre experience or Multinational company environment (desired)

  • Power BI or other BI experience

  • Hyperion or Essbase is an advantage

  • SAP, Oracle, Navision or other similar ERP

  • Systems driven and taste for automation and continuous improvement

  • Matrix team management.

  • Making proposals which apply data and business intelligence.

Qualification/Education

  • Management, Accounting, or any other Finance Degree

Job Description:
We are seeking a Healthcare Analyst to support our hospital’s management team in driving revenue growth through data-driven decision-making. The successful candidate will analyze market trends, optimize pricing strategies, and identify new business opportunities. This role requires a strong analytical mindset and the ability to translate data into actionable business strategies.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Financial Forecasting & Planning: Analyze historical hospital data to model demand for new and existing services. Assist senior management in preparing departmental budgets and revenue projections.

  • Competitive Pricing Strategy: Conduct market research on competitor pricing and service offerings. Recommend competitive and profitable pricing structures for hospital treatments and packages.

  • Quality & Performance Reporting: Compile and analyze operational data to generate reports on service quality and patient satisfaction. Identify trends and propose improvements to enhance the patient experience.

  • Strategic Business Development: Identify and evaluate potential partnerships with insurance providers, corporations, and medical institutions. Prepare business cases and proposals to secure new revenue streams.

  • Marketing Strategy Support: Use data insights to guide the marketing team, ensuring campaigns are targeted effectively to maximize ROI.

Requirements:

  • Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Economics, Finance, Marketing, or a related discipline.

  • Skills & Attributes:

    • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis

    • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and commercially aware

raw_html:
About us
Aspial is a publicly listed company in Singapore, overseeing a diverse portfolio of businesses that include jewellery retail, property development, financial services, hospitality, and resort management. Aspial Lifestyle Business Services (ALBS), located in Malaysia, serves as a shared service business centre, playing a crucial role in supporting the Group’s various operations. The Group features prominent brands such as Maxi-Cash, Lee Hwa, Goldheart Jewellery, and Niessing.

About the role

We are seeking a Business Analyst (BA) to join our dynamic team as a pioneer in Malaysia at Aspial Lifestyle Business Services Sdn Bhd. In this role, you will sit at the bridge between business users and technical teams, ensuring that technology initiatives align with business strategies and enhance productivity.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working across departments, understanding how systems are used in the real world, and turning business problems into practical technology solutions. This role requires flexibility to travel when and as needed.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Requirements Gathering & Analysis: Work with business users to gather, analyse, and document requirements for Change Requests, system enhancements, and internal IT projects.

  • Functional Specification & Design: Translate business needs into clear functional requirements, user stories, workflows, and system specifications. Create wireframes and mock-ups to visualise system changes or new screens for stakeholder alignment.

  • Process Modelling: Develop and maintain business process models (e.g., as-is and to-be flows) to support robust analysis and solution design.

  • Stakeholder Management: Engage stakeholders across different departments to clarify needs, align expectations, and resolve requirement gaps.

  • Lifecycle Support: Support the end-to-end Change Request lifecycle, including requirement gathering, impact analysis, documentation, UAT, and closure.

  • Testing & Validation: Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) by preparing test cases, coordinating testing, tracking defects, and verifying that delivered solutions meet approved business objectives.

  • Project & Compliance Support: Assist the Business Analyst Manager in delivering internal system enhancement and digitalisation projects while ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.

What We're Looking For

Core Competencies

  • Proven ability to produce clear functional documentation, wireframes, and process models.

  • Comfortable engaging and managing multiple stakeholders across business and IT teams.

  • Detail-oriented, analytical, proactive, adaptable, and able to juggle multiple tasks.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment with shifting priorities.

Experience & Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Engineering, or a related field.

  • Minimum 3 years of experience as a Business Analyst, Systems Analyst, or a similar role.

  • Well-versed in navigating software delivery cycles, from initial SDLC planning through to executing successful UAT.

Technical & Analytical Skills

  • Strong systems thinking: able to see the big picture while working through detailed functional and process-level requirements.

  • Good understanding of Database Systems and Data Flow (Knowledge of SQL is a plus).

  • Hands-on experience in POS, ERP, and/or Inventory Management systems is highly preferred.

  • Familiarity with tools such as Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and diagramming/wireframing software (e.g., Visio, Draw.io, Figma, etc.).

job_description: Executive – Business Intelligence We are seeking a responsible, detail-oriented, and proactive Executive to join our Business Intelligence team. In this role, you will support Senior Management and Business Units by delivering business-critical insights, ensuring accurate and timely reporting, and managing data submissions to external regulatory authorities. You will also contribute to strengthening and enhancing the Company’s Business Intelligence capabilities. The key responsibilities include, but are not limited to:Develop and maintain a robust and accurate database to support reporting requirements.Produce, maintain, and continuously enhance reports for various departments.Review data and reports to ensure accuracy, relevance, and reasonableness, and recommend improvements or adjustments where necessary.Liaise with cross-functional teams to verify the accuracy and integrity of data used in reporting.Support the Team Lead in assigned tasks, presentations, and projects as required. Job Specification:Degree in Actuarial Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.Experience in Business Intelligence or Data Analytics is an advantage.Fresh graduates with relevant internship experience, especially in General Insurance, are encouraged to apply.Strong analytical skills with good business and numerical acumen.Effective communication skills, with the ability to present insights clearly to different audiences.Good organizational and project management skills, with a commitment to meeting deadlines.Detail-oriented, proactive, and able to work independently.
raw_html:
Executive – Business Intelligence

 We are seeking a responsible, detail-oriented, and proactive Executive to join our Business Intelligence team. In this role, you will support Senior Management and Business Units by delivering business-critical insights, ensuring accurate and timely reporting, and managing data submissions to external regulatory authorities. You will also contribute to strengthening and enhancing the Company’s Business Intelligence capabilities.

 

The key responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

  1. Develop and maintain a robust and accurate database to support reporting requirements.

  2. Produce, maintain, and continuously enhance reports for various departments.

  3. Review data and reports to ensure accuracy, relevance, and reasonableness, and recommend improvements or adjustments where necessary.

  4. Liaise with cross-functional teams to verify the accuracy and integrity of data used in reporting.

  5. Support the Team Lead in assigned tasks, presentations, and projects as required.

 

Job Specification:

  • Degree in Actuarial Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field.

  • Experience in Business Intelligence or Data Analytics is an advantage.

  • Fresh graduates with relevant internship experience, especially in General Insurance, are encouraged to apply.

  • Strong analytical skills with good business and numerical acumen.

  • Effective communication skills, with the ability to present insights clearly to different audiences.

  • Good organizational and project management skills, with a commitment to meeting deadlines.

  • Detail-oriented, proactive, and able to work independently.
