raw_html:
Please apply until June 30, 2026

Role: Internship (4 to 6 Months)

Location: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia


JTI Talent Internship – Strategic Insights Track


Unlock the power of data with the JTI Talent Internship – Strategic Insights Track at JT International Trading Sdn. Bhd. (JTI Malaysia), an award-winning Top Employer known for its exceptional culture, strong career growth, and global environment. Based in a dynamic and highly regulated corporate setting, you will gain meaningful exposure as part of a high-performing team.



This internship is a hands-on experience where you will work with real data to support business decisions. Your contributions will help translate data into actionable insights that drive commercial performance.


As a JTI Talent Intern in Strategic Insights, you will:


  • Support data consolidation and performance reporting for business tracking

  • Assist in transforming data into clear insights and presentations for internal stakeholders

  • Contribute to market analysis, competitor tracking, and category understanding

  • Support consumer research activities and insight generation

  • Develop skills in data storytelling, analysis, and reporting


Requirements:


  • Candidate with full working rights for Malaysia who is currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree at a recognised university or college

  • Available to commit to the internship for at least 4 months (16 weeks)

  • Studying Business Management, Marketing, Economics, Mathematics/Statistics, Data Science, or any quantitative or research-related fields.

  • Strong analytical and data interpretation skills, with the ability to identify patterns and translate findings into insights

  • Comfortable working with data, spreadsheets, and performance metrics; competent in Excel (pivot tables, formulas, charts) and PowerPoint

  • Communicates effectively in English; proficiency in Bahasa Melayu and/or Mandarin, and prior experience in data analysis or research is an advantage. We are seeking candidates proficient in Mandarin to effectively communicate with Mandarin-speaking clients.


What's in it for you?


You’ll gain hands-on experience working with real business data and learn how insights shape strategic decisions. Guided by experienced professionals, this internship builds the confidence and capability you need to step into your future career.

Job Responsibility:

Report Development and Maintenance

  • Collaborate with key stakeholders to collect and analyze requirements on Dashboard/reports. 

  • Provide in-depth data analysis and dashboard designing.

  • Design, develop and build PBI report to support complex business scenarios. 

  • Maintain and monitor PBI Dashboard on regular basis to ensure data accuracy and completeness.

  • Continue optimizing logic and stability to ensure high performance, scalability, and reliability.

  • Collaborate with cross team, implement relative changes as required

Data Analysis and Monitoring

  • Perform Data Analysis and monitor execution result, provide insightful analysis on operation KPIs. 

  • Take proper action on abnormal performance indicator to ensure Biz Operating healthy 

  • Prepare comprehensive reports (with driver base variance analysis) for management review and support decision making.

  • Conduct regular review and report processes, ensuring key operation indicators are on track and on target Identify opportunities for process improvements and efficiencies with team. 

  • Implement best practices and tools to improve efficiency and enhance reporting and analysis capabilities

Daily Operation Support

  • Conduct regular health check on IT tickets management process to ensure meet compliance requirements.

  • Co-work and collaborate with multiple teams to implement key management directions.

  • Conduct various ad hoc activities as requested by management and key stakeholders to support strategic initiatives

  • Bachelor's Degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Analytics, or related field.

  • 2–3 years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or Reporting roles.

  • Strong hands-on experience with Power BI (dashboard development, data modeling, DAX, Power Query).

  • Proficient in SQL and Microsoft Excel for data analysis and reporting.

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.

  • Good communication and stakeholder management skills.

  • Able to work independently and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams.

  • Experience with process improvement, data quality management, or automation initiatives is an advantage.

raw_html:
JOB PURPOSE

Responsible for maintaining outsourced application systems that have moved beyond the development stage, ensuring that the Company’s systems operate smoothly on a daily basis.


JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

Support Project Management & Service Management on outsourced systems:

  • Participate in the project and development of the systems.

  • To provide support and implement change requests for outsourced systems to meet users’ requirements.

  • Assist project management to track the project milestones and deliverables of the project. Track and follow up on the issues raised during the project phase and ensure all issues are being addressed promptly.

  • Ensure adequate education and knowledge transfer to business users for outsourced systems applications.

  • Understanding/conducting user requirements and translate into system requirement document.

  • Mapping and documenting interfaces between legacy and new systems.

  • To follow up closely with vendors for temporary workaround as well as permanent resolution, drawing up, supervising and documenting testing schedules for complete systems.

  • Planning and working flexibly to deadlines.

  • Providing training and user manuals to users of a new system.


Problem Solving & Issues Tracking on Outsourced systems

  • To provide first-level support for problem solving & issue-tracking for outsourced systems.

  • Identify and tracking of issues reported for outsourced systems and liaising with users on daily BAU.

  • Analyzing the existing systems and Identify risk that may directly impact to the outsourced systems as well as to business users to control and mitigate the risk to the systems.

  • Identifying options for potential solutions and assessing them for both technical and business suitability.

  • Working closely with colleagues, developers, testers and a variety of end users to ensure technical compatibility and user satisfaction.

  • Supporting users on change control and system updates.

  • Keeping up to date with technical and industry developments.


Rules & Regulations and Policies & Procedures Compliance

  • Ensure adherence to internal policies & guidelines and all relevant regulations.


Staff Development and Engagement

  • Monitor staff performance, development plan, discipline and performance matters/issues.

  • Provide coaching and mentoring to direct reports.


JOB REQUIREMENTS

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, Computer Science, or a related field.

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience and welcome for fresh graduates

  • Prior working experience in financial institutions is required.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).

  • Strong understanding of the System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) within a fast‑paced financial industry environment.

  • Well versed in technologies supporting various investment platform channels.

  • Experience in the stock broking industry is an advantage.

raw_html:
About Us

Cyan Renewables (rebranded from MMA Offshore in July 2025) is a leading provider of marine and subsea services supporting the global energy transition. With a fleet of 35 specialised vessels, Cyan is a trusted partner to the world’s leading energy and offshore wind developers/contractors, providing comprehensive services across Asia, Europe and Australia.


Why work with us?

At Cyan Renewables group, you will join a diverse, experienced and forward-thinking team of global marine and subsea professionals. Whether onshore or offshore – we empower our team to build an exceptional career amongst our industry’s top professionals and technical experts.    

  • Opportunity with an Industry Leader

  • Full-time permanent role

  • Exposure to a wide variety of offshore activities


About this Opportunity

We are looking for a Business Systems Analyst to gather, analyse, and document requirements, ensuring that enterprise application and technology projects align with the organization's objectives. Based in Kula Lumpur Malaysia and reporting to the General Manager – Digital Enablement, you will work closely with project teams and cross-functional user groups to translate business requirements into technical specifications and support the successful delivery of technology solutions.


Key Responsibilities

  • Collaborate with stakeholders to elicit and document business and functional requirements.

  • Analyse business processes, workflows, and user needs to identify opportunities for improvement.

  • Define and prioritise requirements based on business value and feasibility.

  • Create clear and detailed requirement documents, including user stories, use cases, process flows, and mock-ups.

  • Ensure that requirements are well-documented, understood, and approved by stakeholders.

  • Translate business requirements into functional system specifications.

  • Review and validate technical solutions to ensure they meet business needs and requirements.

  • Facilitate communication between business stakeholders and technical teams to ensure a shared understanding of project goals.

  • Serve as a liaison between business users, development teams, vendors/partners, by translating business needs into technical terms.

  • Assist in supporting SMEs in developing test scenarios and test cases to validate that IT solutions meet requirements.

  • Assess the impact of proposed changes on business processes and stakeholders.

  • Analyse data to identify trends, patterns, and insights that can inform business decisions.

  • Provide input for process improvements based on insights gained from business analysis activities.

  • Ensuring that enterprise solutions comply with relevant regulations and standards, and that data is stored, presented, and handled securely in line within the company’s ISMS and IBMS governance requirements.


Qualifications and Experience

  • Bachelor in Computer Science (or equivalent IT related field)

  • Certification in Project Management (PRINCE2, PMP, or equivalent experience)

  • Knowledge of best practices in data integration and automation.

  • Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills to understand complex business processes and propose effective solutions.

  • Understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) methodologies such as Agile, Scrum, or Waterfall.

  • Knowledge of database concepts, SQL queries, and data analysis tools.

  • Familiarity with system design principles and ability to understand technical architecture diagrams.

  • Proficiency in creating various types of documentation including business requirements documents (BRD), functional requirements documents (FRD), BPMN diagrams use cases, user stories, etc.

  • Excellent communication skills for effectively translating technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders and vice versa.

  • Requirements gathering and documentation expertise, including the ability to create clear and comprehensive requirement artifacts.


How to Apply

At Cyan Renewables Group, we strive to foster a diverse and inclusive environment that empowers all employees to strive for their full potential. We strongly encourage applications from people of all backgrounds.

To apply for this role, please submit your CV by 09 July 2026. Please note, we will commence shortlisting prior to the closing date.


No agencies please.

raw_html:
Job Responsibilities

  1. Develop and execute product operation strategies to improve key metrics, including user acquisition, retention, engagement, conversion, and repurchase.

  2. Collaborate with the marketing team to plan and implement operational campaigns based on business objectives, including user acquisition, activation, re-engagement, referral programs, membership programs, and loyalty initiatives.

  3. Monitor and analyze product performance data and user behavior, and provide recommendations for product optimization and user experience improvement.

  4. Track project progress and coordinate closely with Product, UI Design, Engineering, QA, and Marketing teams to ensure timely delivery and successful launches.

  5. Establish and maintain an operational data framework and prepare regular operation analysis reports.

  6. Gather user feedback and competitor insights to continuously optimize products and operational strategies.

  7. Support user engagement initiatives through CRM, push notifications, SMS, email campaigns, and other communication channels.

  8. Independently prepare and maintain various documents, including reports, requirement documents, PRDs, SOPs, and operational guidelines.

Requirements

  1. Minimum 3 years of experience in Product Operations, Internet Operations, or Game Operations.

  2. Familiar with Internet products such as Mobile Apps, H5 applications, and Mini Programs.

  3. Strong understanding of operational methodologies and capable of conducting data analysis, funnel analysis, retention analysis, and user behavior analysis.

  4. Possess strong analytical thinking and data-driven decision-making capabilities.

  5. Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills, with the ability to drive projects independently.

  6. Proactive, detail-oriented, and capable of working effectively in a fast-paced environment.

  7. Proficiency in Chinese is required as Chinese is the primary working language for communication with cross-border teams and stakeholders.

  8. Experience in Internet, gaming, or digital platform industries will be an advantage.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in CRM operations, user growth, or lifecycle management.

  • Experience with operational analytics tools and data visualization platforms.

  • Familiar with user segmentation, membership systems, and retention strategies.

  • Experience working in multicultural or cross-border teams is preferred.

Address
Near MRT Ampang Park Station

raw_html:
Company Overview
Established in 1998, Neo Innovative Tech (M) Sdn. Bhd., formerly Chiyoda Software Laboratory (M) Sdn. Bhd., has over 27 years of experience in IT consultancy and system development. In 2024, Neo Innovative Tech became part of the A-1 Bakery Group, providing IT operational support while developing practical business, retail, manufacturing, and ERP-related solutions.

About the Role
We are looking for an ERP Business Analyst with strong system understanding and technical awareness. The candidate will work closely with business users and developers to study requirements, analyze processes, design system solutions, support testing, and ensure successful system delivery.

This role is suitable for someone with around 5 years of experience in business analysis, system analysis, ERP implementation, or business application support.

Key Responsibilities
1. Conduct requirement studies with business users and stakeholders.
2. Analyze business processes, workflows, system gaps, and improvement areas.
3. Prepare functional specifications, process flows, user stories, and system documentation.
4. Work with the development team to design system modules, database structures, workflows, and integrations.
5. Support system architecture planning and provide technical input during solution design.
6. Coordinate with developers to ensure requirements are clearly understood and implemented correctly.
7. Prepare test scenarios, support system testing, UAT, troubleshooting, and issue resolution.
8. Assist in ERP, MRP, inventory, purchasing, production, sales, retail, or related business system projects.
9. Support system rollout, user training, documentation, and post-implementation support.
10. Monitor project progress and coordinate communication between business users and technical teams.

Requirements
1. Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business IT, or related field.
2. Around 5 years of experience in business analysis, system analysis, ERP implementation, or software-related projects.
3. Experience in requirement gathering, business process mapping, gap analysis, and system documentation.
4. Good understanding of ERP, MRP, inventory, purchasing, production, sales, or other business application systems.
5. Able to understand system architecture, database concepts, APIs, integrations, and application workflows.
6. Experience in system testing, UAT coordination, issue tracking, and production support.
7. Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, and documentation skills.
8. Able to communicate effectively with both business users and technical teams.

Added Advantage
1. Experience with PHP, Laravel, CodeIgniter, Symfony, or similar web frameworks.
2. Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, or frontend frameworks such as Vue.js, React, or Nuxt.js.
3. Experience with databases such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, MSSQL, or similar relational databases.
4. Familiarity with RESTful APIs, web services, third-party integrations, and system interfaces.
5. Experience with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, or similar version control tools.
6. Knowledge of SDLC, Agile, Waterfall, project documentation, and change management.
7. Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.
8. Experience in manufacturing, bakery, retail, distribution, or trading business systems.

What We Offer
1. Competitive salary and benefits package.
2. Exposure to end-to-end system implementation and business process improvement.
3. Collaborative and dynamic work environment.
4. Transport and carpark allowance.
5. Medical claim entitlement.
6. Career growth opportunities in business analysis, system design, and project leadership.

raw_html:
Role Purpose 

The CRM & Market Intelligence role drives data-led CRM strategy, reporting, and insights, ensuring a single source of truth for customer performance and enabling informed commercial decisions across Marketing, Trade, and Operations.  

This role bridges data → insights → action, supporting CRM growth (MSP, Active Rate, CLV) and overall business performance. 

 

Key Responsibilities 

1. CRM Reporting & Insights 

  • Own recurring CRM performance reporting (MSP, Active Rate, Member Sales, etc.) 

  • Translate data into clear insights and business recommendations 

  • Deliver management-ready decks and performance summaries 

  • Conduct campaign post-mortems and effectiveness analysis 

 

2. Dashboard & Data Management 

  • Build and maintain Power BI dashboards for CRM and commercial tracking 

  • Ensure data accuracy, governance, and consistency across systems 

  • Act as the single source of truth for CRM metrics and definitions 

 

3. CRM Campaign & Lifecycle Support 

  • Support segmentation, targeting, and customer lifecycle strategies (CLV, RFM, journeys) 

  • Evaluate campaign performance and recommend optimization opportunities 

  • Align CRM communications (EDM, push, WhatsApp, etc.) with insights 

 

4. Commercial & Market Intelligence 

  • Analyze customer behavior, shopping patterns, and category trends 

  • Identify:  New customer acquisition impact, cannibalizations / cross-category behavior, growth opportunities by segment/store/category , act as a data consultant to different stakeholders 

 

5. Stakeholder & Project Support 

  • Work closely with Marketing, Trade, Operations, IT and regional teams 

  • Support CRM/CLV projects and cross-functional initiatives 

  • Provide insights for decision-making in campaigns, partnerships, and promotions 

 

Success Metrics (What “Good” Looks Like) 

  • Accuracy and reliability of CRM reporting 

  • Actionable insights adopted by stakeholders 

  • Improvement in key KPIs:  Member Sales Participation (MSP) , Active Rate, CRM-driven sales contribution, Timely delivery of dashboards and analysis 

 

Skills & Requirements 

  • Strong analytical skills (Excel, PowerPoint, Power BI preferred) 

  • Ability to translate data into clear business storytelling 

  • Understanding of CRM concepts (segmentation, lifecycle, CLV) 

  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills 

  • Attention to detail and data accuracy 

  • Preferably with exposure to O+O (Online + Offline) CRM projects 

  • Experience in brand loyalty programs 

  • Familiarity with commercial / retail performance analysis

About Hytech:

Hytech is a leading financial technology company specializing in cutting-edge financial technology solutions. Our innovative platforms and applications empower our clients to manage their finances efficiently, securely, and with unparalleled convenience. As a market leader in the financial technology industry, we are dedicated to driving digital transformation and shaping the future of financial technology. Our passion for connecting the dots extends beyond the technological realm. We foster a culture of inclusivity that values collaboration and knowledge-sharing among our team members, clients, and partners. Together, we harness the power of connection to inspire innovation, drive growth, and shape a better future for the financial technology industry.

Join us at Hytech as we continue our journey of connecting the dots and paving the way to success for our clients and partners. Let us embark on a collaborative adventure, where we explore new possibilities, drive innovation, and navigate the exciting landscape of the digital world together.


About the Role :

We're looking for a Business Analyst to join our Detection Team, where you'll leverage large-scale data analysis and behavioral clustering to identify coordinated fraudulent activities and hidden risk patterns. Your work will strengthen risk controls, automate detection processes, and support data-driven decision-making.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Analyze user data to identify suspicious account clusters.
  • Detect fraud patterns, arbitrage, and coordinated account behaviors.
  • Develop and optimize data-driven risk detection rules.
  • Build automated monitoring workflows and analytical dashboards.
  • Partner with cross-functional teams to improve risk controls.


What We’re Looking For:

  • 2+ years of experience in Data Analytics, BI, or Risk Operations.
  • Strong SQL skills; Python or R is a plus.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
  • Experience working with large datasets and behavioral analysis.
  • Professional English proficiency.


Working Location:

  • KL Eco City (accessible via LRT Abdullah Hukum)

 

Working Hours:

  • 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Monday - Friday

job_description: Responsibilities:Conducting in-depth, timely and critical analysis of listed companies and their underlying businesses. In addition, you need to undertake comprehensive supply chain research to gain a holistic understanding of the business beyond the numbers and to derive a company’s intrinsic value.Monitoring industry developments and trends and be prepared to give quick, precise opinion on a specific stock when requested.Interpreting data and analysis on market, economic and political events.Generating stock selection ideas to the Fund Managers.Requirements:Have at least a strong Bachelor’s Degree, backed by a solid theoretical foundation in your major. While qualifications in Accountancy, Finance or Management are useful, graduates with other specialisations are encouraged to apply, as we can be pretty open-minded.Apart from strong business analytical skills, you must also possess excellent written and spoken communication skills - fluency in Mandarin would be a huge asset.You thrive on challenge, can cope with tight deadlines, and have an outgoing and strong personality that can take on setbacks.All Equity Analysts will need to obtain the necessary regulatory licenses.
raw_html:
Responsibilities:

  • Conducting in-depth, timely and critical analysis of listed companies and their underlying businesses. In addition, you need to undertake comprehensive supply chain research to gain a holistic understanding of the business beyond the numbers and to derive a company’s intrinsic value.

  • Monitoring industry developments and trends and be prepared to give quick, precise opinion on a specific stock when requested.

  • Interpreting data and analysis on market, economic and political events.

  • Generating stock selection ideas to the Fund Managers.

Requirements:

  • Have at least a strong Bachelor’s Degree, backed by a solid theoretical foundation in your major. While qualifications in Accountancy, Finance or Management are useful, graduates with other specialisations are encouraged to apply, as we can be pretty open-minded.

  • Apart from strong business analytical skills, you must also possess excellent written and spoken communication skills - fluency in Mandarin would be a huge asset.

  • You thrive on challenge, can cope with tight deadlines, and have an outgoing and strong personality that can take on setbacks.

  • All Equity Analysts will need to obtain the necessary regulatory licenses.

raw_html:
Company Description

About SEEK

At SEEK, we serve a noble purpose: to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and to help organisations succeed.

By joining us, you’ll be part of a multi-national technology business that is far-reaching with a start-up working culture that focuses on a set of collaborative values and appreciates dynamic cultures. SEEK is a place where potential meets possibility – it’s where your career aspiration and our purpose can make great things happen.

Why join us?

Be part of a multi-national tech company with strong core values to help us solve complex challenges for our customers while building a flexible, exciting career – one that could take you anywhere.

We’re looking for people who thrive in dynamic teams and enjoy tackling regular new challenges in a large-scale, modern, and agile environment. As one of Asia Pacific’s largest tech companies, you’ll contribute to multi-lingual systems, solving high-impact problems to the benefit of millions of people within the region.

Be part of a fast-growing company with a strong culture and clear purpose, innovation and strong investment in responsible AI. SEEK offers you the opportunity to be involved in impactful work and be part of a great team. Expect a great investment in your career development with access to workshops, conferences and courses while you work alongside seasoned experts.

Job Description

About the role

As a member of the Data Services Team, you'll develop databases and data processing pipelines that power predictive analytics, data science, and business intelligence across SEEK. Reporting to Data Services Management, you'll collaborate with Business Analysts, Data Analysts, and development teams to design technical solutions that meet business objectives.

You'll architect data processing pipelines, data warehouses, datamarts, and dimensional models on AWS infrastructure. This includes designing database schemas, optimizing SQL queries, and developing automation scripts. You'll also support end users with data discovery, query tuning, and resolving data quality issues while ensuring platform performance and scalability.

Working closely with AI & Ontology, BI and Reporting, Strategy and Product teams, and Infrastructure teams, your success will be measured by delivering data solutions that enable informed, data-driven decisions across SEEK.

Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and test data processing pipelines, data warehouses, datamarts, and dimensional models.
  • Create optimized database schemas, indexes, views, and SQL queries on AWS infrastructure.
  • Provide technical support for data discovery, query optimization, and data quality issues. Analyze and optimize database performance, SQL queries, and ETL processes.
  • Deliver operational support including incident response, CI/CD implementation, monitoring, alerting, and database administration.
Qualifications

Essential Skills and experience

  • Proven experience with data warehouses, datamarts, and dimensional data modeling.
  • Advanced SQL development and database performance optimization.
  • Strong software engineering skills with proficiency in coding and scripting for data processing.
  • Experience building data pipelines, ETL processes, and orchestration workflows.
  • Hands-on experience with big data systems (Hadoop/EMR, Spark/Presto, MPP databases).
  • Working knowledge of AWS services, particularly EMR and S3.

Desirable Skills and experience

  • Machine Learning Engineering experience.
  • Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, monitoring, and operational support.
  • Database and system administration background.
Additional Information

Perks and benefits

At SEEK we offer:

  • Tailored career development planning 
  • Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role
  • Annual Performance Bonus Plan
  • SEEK is committed to operating sustainably and is preparing for the impacts of climate change and the transition to a low-carbon future, and is working to minimise its environmental impacts which includes a long-term emissions reduction target of net zero by FY2050
  • The opportunity to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per financial year

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us. Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those with eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

We aim to design & develop a detailed, human centered experiences to positively impact the lives of millions of people.Join us as a part of AIA Group today!Requirements:University / College graduate in computer science or other relevant discipline.Good understanding of how system works.Possess knowledge of application developments methodologies or system configurations3 to 5 years’ relevant experience in Workday regional solution/system support.Has exposure in Workday modules BAU support. Experience with any HR Workday modules is a plus.Familiar with ITSM tool (ServiceNow) & SLA.Practical experience with HR processes and practices.Consistent and proactive in BAU operations and deliverables.Roles & Responsibilities:Support L2 day-to-day operations, addressing tickets, incidents and support requests related to Workday and HR applicationsEnsure resolution time meeting SLA target.Self-discipline and good deliverables to promote accountability and trust at work.Perform system configuration and customization.Monitor system notification (errors) and take appropriate actions.Support end users in system usage and assist in raising overall HRIT Platform proficiency in the end user base.Support Manager in executing proof of concepts (POCs) for new Change Request when necessary.Participate in regional project activities with HRIT focus.Successful system and process integration, efficient SLA driven end-user support and communications.Liaises with necessary AIA colleagues or business partners outside HRIT to ensure delivery of the functional work as required.Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements.Manage S1 issue escalation with relevant parties effectively.Ensure solutions are consistent with global standards and ensure global solutions architecture team is aligned
raw_html:
Why Us?

In AIA Digital+, we serve as AIA’s Group-led Technology, Digital & Analytics hub by evolving innovative technology to shape the future. We aim to design & develop a detailed, human centered experiences to positively impact the lives of millions of people.

Join us as a part of AIA Group today!


Requirements:

  • University / College graduate in computer science or other relevant discipline.

  • Good understanding of how system works.

  • Possess knowledge of application developments methodologies or system configurations

  • 3 to 5 years’ relevant experience in Workday regional solution/system support.

  • Has exposure in Workday modules BAU support. Experience with any HR Workday modules is a plus.

  • Familiar with ITSM tool (ServiceNow) & SLA.

  • Practical experience with HR processes and practices.

  • Consistent and proactive in BAU operations and deliverables.


Roles & Responsibilities:

  • Support L2 day-to-day operations, addressing tickets, incidents and support requests related to Workday and HR applications

  • Ensure resolution time meeting SLA target.

  • Self-discipline and good deliverables to promote accountability and trust at work.

  • Perform system configuration and customization.

  • Monitor system notification (errors) and take appropriate actions.

  • Support end users in system usage and assist in raising overall HRIT Platform proficiency in the end user base.

  • Support Manager in executing proof of concepts (POCs) for new Change Request when necessary.

  • Participate in regional project activities with HRIT focus.

  • Successful system and process integration, efficient SLA driven end-user support and communications.

  • Liaises with necessary AIA colleagues or business partners outside HRIT to ensure delivery of the functional work as required.

  • Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements.

  • Manage S1 issue escalation with relevant parties effectively.

  • Ensure solutions are consistent with global standards and ensure global solutions architecture team is aligned

job_description: Position Objective:To become a pivotal part of Group Data Governance by partnering with 18+ local business units across Asia Pacific and Group functions (Operations, Healthcare, raw_html:
Position Objective:

To become a pivotal part of Group Data Governance by partnering with 18+ local business units across Asia Pacific and Group functions (Operations, Healthcare, Investment, Finance and Human Resources) to deliver best data quality and governance for both structured and unstructured data

Roles and Responsibilities

This role will be based in Malaysia with a Groupwide scope and acts as an extension of the Group Data Governance team to independently support business units and Group functions on data governance.

The key responsibilities of this role include the following:

Group Data Governance Standard and Guidelines

·       To support 18+ business units and Group functions (Operations, Healthcare, Investment, Finance and Human Resources) in implementing the Group Data Governance Standard

·       To support the review, revision and expansion of the Group Data Governance Standard

·       To develop guidelines on data governance, e.g. reference data management and data format definitions

Unstructured Data Governance

·       To support new initiatives to expand the scope of data governance to unstructured data (e.g. critical documents) in addition to structured data (CDEs)

Data Audits, Risks and Controls

·       To support audits related to data governance and quality at Group and business unit levels

·       To prepare data quality key risk indicators for reporting

·       To support data risk and control assessments

Data Quality Issue Management

·       To enhance and maintain the data quality issue management (DQIM) module

·       To enhance and produce monthly Group data quality issue management dashboards

·       To review data quality metrics and investigate data quality issues to understand their root causes and follow up remediation plans with business units

·       To support the review and revision of data quality thresholds for identifying data quality issues

Groupwide Data Governance Tooling

·       To manage and upgrade the Groupwide data governance tool and VM

·       To research, identify and assess data governance tools, including those to govern structured and unstructured data, and with AI capabilities

Groupwide Data Dictionary of Critical Data Element (CDE) Definitions

·       To maintain and enhance the Groupwide data dictionary of CDE definitions, including CDEs, data quality rules, reference data, data quality thresholds, data domains, data owners, database mapping, PII labels, data classifications, metadata and business glossary, etc.

·       To support Group data owner functions in their reviews of CDE definitions to ensure their adequate coverage

·       To support Group data owner functions in defining enhanced and additional CDEs as well as data quality rules, e.g. for health clinical coding data standardisation

·       To support reference data management

Monthly Data Quality Assessments

·       To support business units on setting and monitoring data quality implementation targets

·       To enhance the Group frameworks for data quality assessments for business units and Group functions’ adoption

·       To support and upskill business units and Group functions on implementing and running monthly data quality assessments on CDEs

·       To collect and consolidate data quality metrics from business units and Group functions

·       To enhance and produce Group data quality dashboards

To review business units’ data quality implementation and data quality metrics


Financial and Non-Financial Measures:

·       BUs and Group functions’ compliance with the Group Data Governance Standard New metrics for unstructured data governance

·       Audit observations relating to data governance and quality

·       CDE Implementation%

·       Data Quality%

·       Number of Data Quality Issues

·       TDA Health Data Richness KPI (data quality)

·    CDE coverage reviews


Communication Requirements

·       To coordinate and follow up with business units and Group functions on data governance matters, including alignments across parties

·       To work with Group Data Governance colleagues across geographical locations

·   To liaise with external vendors on data governance tooling and VM


Job Requirements:

Education – University graduate or above in IT, Computer Science or related disciplines

Experience – 5-10 years of hands-on experience of working on data governance or related data areas independently using data tools based on business needs and regulatory requirements

Technical Skills –

·       Experience of governing unstructured data, e.g. critical documents and images

·       Experience of independently setting up, upgrading and managing data governance and data quality assessment software, preferably Data360 Govern and Analyze or equivalent tools, and VM

·       Experience of independently building and maintaining data dictionaries on data governance platforms, including CDEs, data quality rules, reference data, data quality thresholds, data domains, data owners, database mapping, PII labels, data classifications, metadata and business glossary, etc.

·       Experience of independently developing and performing data quality assessments using data quality assessment tools

·       Experience of independently developing and producing regular data quality dashboards using PowerBI or equivalent tools

·       Python

·       Unix

·       PowerPoint

·       Excel

Other Specific Skills –

·       Good verbal and written communications

·       Teamworking - experience of working remotely with overseas counterparts preferred

·       Ability to work independently

·       Analytical mindset

·       Project management experience preferred

·       Can do attitude

·       Quick learner

·       Attention to details

·       Insurance industry or banking experience preferred

·       Knowledge of health data preferred, especially clinical coding data standards

raw_html:
Job Description

  • Responsible for Group Financial Planning, Group Budgeting, and Group Forecasting activities.

  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives by enhancing existing processes and providing financial guidance and recommendations to Business Units through sound management analysis and present the associated financial implications.

  • Perform job costing analysis and ensure accurate cost estimation for projects.

  • Responsible for submission of quotes on job basis.

  • Perform evaluation of supplier’s proposal including technical analysis in the cost estimations.

  • Collaborate with Sourcing, Engineering, Sales teams, and customers to establish accurate product pricing and resolve discrepancies, missing information, and technical issues related to drawings and builds.

  • Prepare Monthly Revenue Closing Analysis Report and discuss with factory if necessary.

  • Prepare and analyse Group financial performance.

  • Support team members with ad-hoc financial analysis and reporting requirements.

  • Perform any other duties and responsibilities as assigned by the Finance Manager and/or Head of Finance.

Job Requirement

  • Degree in Finance/Accounting or professional qualification in Accounting & Finance e.g. CIMA, ACCA, etc. Candidate who are pursuing professional accounting degree is welcome to apply.

  • 2–5 years of relevant working experience in similar role.

  • Strong proficiency in English, Mandarin and Bahasa Malaysia. Fluent communication in Mandarin is an advantage.

  • Strong spreadsheet proficiency and experience in utilizing computerized accounting/ ERP.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel for reporting and analysis table and PowerPoint for presentations.

  • Responsible, organized, and able to meet deadlines.

Company Benefits

  • Annual Leave, Medical Leave, Birthday Leave, Exam Leave, Hospitalization Leave, Maternity Leave etc.

  • Medical Insurance: Group Hospitalization & Surgical Insurance, Group Term Life Insurance

  • Statutory Contribution: EPF, SOCSO and SOCSO EIS

  • Bonus, Annual Increment, Promotion (Non-contractual and at sole discretion of Company)

  • Medical Check-up

  • Medical Claim, Dental Claim, Optical Claim, Mileage Claim

  • Transportation Allowance, Outstation Allowance, Newborn Allowance etc

  • Internal and External Training

  • Staff Welfares: Monthly Staff Birthday Celebration, Sports Activities, Festival Celebration, Team Building, Company Annual Event

  • Company Polo-T, Company Uniform, Company Jacket

  • Appropriate Pantry Essentials: Microwave, Refrigerator, Coffee Machine, Water Purifier, Snacks

  • Entertainment Area: Pool Table, Foosball Table, Switch

raw_html:
Role Overview

SEAFIC is seeking an experienced economic researcher (8–10 years’ experience) to conduct and manage research projects across three research pillars, contributing to regional thought leadership. This role is suitable for an established researcher with a strong economic policy background, regional perspective and proven track record in research output and research project leadership experience.

Key Responsibilities

  • Research Strategy & Thought Leadership: Design and develop original research questions, thematic priorities, and a forward-looking research pipeline aligned with SEAFIC's strategic research agenda across economic development, trade and investment, and international cooperation in ASEAN. Identify emerging regional issues and conceptualise high-impact, policy-relevant research initiatives.

  • Research Management & Publications: Manage complex research projects from inception to publication, producing high-quality outputs including flagship reports, policy briefs, white papers, commentaries, and other research publications that inform policy and regional discourse.

  • Research Excellence & Innovation: Apply research methodologies, including quantitative analysis, foresight techniques, economic modelling, and data analytics, to ensure research outputs meet international standards of quality and credibility.

  • Policy Engagement & External Representation: Represent SEAFIC in policy dialogues, roundtables, conferences, and engagements with government agencies, international organisations, think tanks, academia, and the private sector. Translate research findings into practical policy recommendations for diverse stakeholders.

  • Research Mentorship & Collaboration: Mentor and supervise researchers and interns, fostering a culture of research excellence, integrity, collaboration, and continuous learning while ensuring the quality of institutional research outputs.

  • Strategic Partnerships & Resource Mobilisation: Develop collaborative research partnerships and contribute to securing external funding through research proposals, grants, and commissioned projects.

Key Requirements

  • Minimum 8-10 years experience in economic research, preferably within a think tank, government agency, regional or international organisation, academic institution, or policy research environment.

  • PhD in economics, econometrics, applied economics and any other related discipline.

  • Demonstrated experience in designing original research questions, developing strategic research agendas, and managing a pipeline of policy-relevant research projects.

  • Proven track record of producing high-quality research publications, including flagship reports, policy papers, white papers, or peer-reviewed publications.

  • Strong quantitative research skills, including experience in collecting, extracting, analysing, and interpreting primary and secondary data.

  • Experience with foresight analysis, economic modelling, big data analytics, or other advanced research methodologies.

  • Experience engaging with policymakers, senior government officials, international organisations, business leaders, or regional institutions.

  • Strong understanding of Malaysia and ASEAN's economic development, trade and investment, regional integration, and emerging geopolitical and technological trends.

  • Excellent analytical, writing, presentation, and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to communicate complex research to both technical and non-technical audiences.

  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple research projects independently while mentoring junior researchers and collaborating across multidisciplinary teams.

raw_html:
Fragomen is looking for a Pricing Analytic Specialist to join our Commercial team. This role is based in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia supporting the business globally.

The Pricing Analytic Specialist supports Fragomen’s commercial and pricing strategy by delivering accurate, timely, and data-driven pricing solutions. Working closely with legal, finance, and business teams, this role contributes to client proposals, pricing governance, and commercial insights that drive revenue performance.

For priority consideration, please apply directly via the Fragomen Careers Page:

https://fragomen.wd115.myworkdayjobs.com/en-US/FragomenCareers/job/Pricing-Specialist-II_REQ-026124?locations=79ffdae5b05b106443f7cdc43•••687d

As a Pricing Specialist, you will…

  • Manage and prioritize incoming pricing and commercial requests, ensuring timely turnaround in line with business needs;

  • Support the development of pricing proposals, including RFP responses, client bids, and ad hoc commercial requests;

  • Collaborate with internal stakeholders to develop pricing solutions aligned with client requirements and firm strategy;

  • Assist in structuring fee arrangements that reflect the value delivered to clients;
    Maintain and update pricing databases, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and completeness of data;

  • Record and track pricing proposals, approvals, and commercial terms across internal systems;

  • Support compliance with internal pricing policies and approval frameworks;
    Track contractual obligations, including service level agreements (SLAs) and performance commitments;

  • Analyze pricing data, trends, and client behaviors to support commercial decision-making;

  • Develop reports and dashboards to monitor pricing performance and key initiatives;

  • Evaluate pricing strategies and identify opportunities for continuous improvement.

An ideal candidate should…

  • Demonstrate strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills;

  • Be able to manage multiple priorities and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment;

  • Communicate effectively and build strong relationships with stakeholders;

  • Demonstrate commercial awareness and an understanding of pricing strategies;

  • Be proactive, responsive, and client-focused;

  • Be comfortable working across teams in a collaborative, global environment.

Let’s talk if you have…

  • A Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, Economics, or a related field;
    2–5 years of experience in pricing, finance, commercial operations, or a related role;

  • Experience in professional services, legal services, or consulting environments is preferred;

  • Proficiency in Excel, SharePoint, and data analysis tools;

  • Familiarity with CRM systems or pricing/financial tools is an advantage.

At Fragomen, we offer excellent training and the opportunity to work with knowledgeable professionals across a collaborative global network.

Finding the right person is very important to us. We hope that finding the right place is important to you.

Interested? Apply now. Don’t forget to include your CV.

All offers and/or employment contracts are contingent upon the successful completion of the Firm’s pre-employment screening process. This process may include verifying the candidate’s identity, confirming legal authorization to work in the offered position’s location, and conducting a comprehensive background check, where permitted by local regulations. We use limited AI‑assisted tools for administrative screening purposes only - never for decision‑making. All hiring decisions are made by people. Applicants may have rights to information and explanations regarding the use of such tools, or request human review, as required by applicable regional laws.

Our Privacy Policy is available at https://www.libertyspecialtymarketsap.com/footer/privacy-policy/. By submitting an application for this role, you confirm that you have read, understood and consent to our Privacy Policy and further, that we may store any information you provide in the cloud and/or a third-party platform used by the Liberty Mutual Group. You further agree that we may store your information for a period of up to 5 years and that we may contact you about relevant employment opportunities with the Liberty Mutual Group.
raw_html:
Quality Assurance Analyst (Cantonese Speaking)

We are looking for a Quality Assurance Analyst to join our team to support initiatives for the business to meet established quality standards through regular quality review of processes. The role analyses trends for improvement opportunities and service efficiencies.

Do you have experience in Quality Assurance within the Insurance industry, or have you previously worked in an Underwriting Processing Services Team, and would love the opportunity to work in a dynamic environment to help drive business improvement?

Do you like the sound of joining a company with a proven track record of promoting from within, and who puts a real focus on development & growth opportunities through structured training & induction sessions, professional skills training, project opportunities?

If so, read on to find out more!


Our Company

At Liberty, we take a long-term view, and part of that is building genuine relationships, both internally with colleagues and externally with our clients and brokers. Our people have the opportunity to make a real impact; grow their careers, recognize their potential, celebrate their uniqueness, and provide them with the knowledge and environment to succeed.

Liberty's leadership team and management have the stability, experience, and foresight to guide us through the ups and downs of the financial cycle while empowering our teams to focus on market relevance and delivering quality customer service. Other benefits include:

  • Wellness and Inclusion – We offer a range of tools to support employee well-being and diversity, inclusion, and equity. This includes health webinars, EAP guidance, generous parental leave, fresh fruits, and more.

  • Career Development – We offer opportunities for promotions from within, extensive technical and non-technical training, secondment opportunities, and more.

  • Community and Environment – our charities align themselves with the Liberty philosophy of helping people live safer and more secure lives, and all Liberty employees are actively encouraged to get involved in giving back to the community. Whether that means participating in company-organized volunteering opportunities, attending fund-raising events, or using your extra paid day per year of service leave dedicated to community service.


Some of the key responsibilities include:

  • Conducts monthly quality reviews/audits according on stipulated timeframe/SLA while complying to process SOPs and GRS QA Framework & guidelines.

  • Validate data accuracy, completeness, and alignment with business and regulatory standards.

  • Analyze error trends, identify root causes, and suggest corrective actions or process improvements.

  • Document QA findings and maintain accurate quality logs and reports.

  • Provide timely feedback to BPO vendor partners teams and support capability uplift.

  • Participate in calibration sessions with onshore counterparts and BPO QA teams.

  • Act as a subject matter expert (SME) on underwriting procedures, business rules, and quality governance.

  • Provide support in creating and maintenance of QA calibration guide for all existing process and new transition process.

  • Resolve dispute in rebuttal according to action plan agreed by the team in time.

  • Log error tracked and follow-up on the correction by the processor.

  • Participate in any training session from business stakeholder and update QA SOP to align with the change.

  • Responsible in new starter training as mentor and dedicated trainer for different processes.

  • Undertake any ad hoc tasks and responsibilities as and when required.

  • Maintaining a highly effective and responsive working relationship with all internal and external stakeholders.


Qualifications & Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in commerce, Business Administration, or a related field.

  • Fluent in reading and understanding Cantonese language to manage Hong Kong stakeholders.

  • 5 –8 years of experience in P&C insurance operations, with a strong focus on underwriting for APAC markets.

  • Minimum 2+ years of audit, quality assurance, or process excellence experience, preferably in a BPO or shared service environment.

  • Familiarity with multiple lines of business within commercial or specialty insurance (e.g., Property, Casualty, Marine, Terrorism, Financial Lines, etc.)

  • Strong understanding of compliance, control, and documentation standards in underwriting processes.

  • Preferred background in shared services or BPO environments as Quality Auditor of global insurance back-office processes. And experience working with BPO vendors teams such as Genpact.


Key Skills

  • Cantonese language expertise

  • High attention to detail and accuracy.

  • Ability to interpret and apply underwriting guidelines, documentation and identify control gaps.

  • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills across geographies and teams.

  • Working knowledge of policy administration systems, workflow tools, and audit platforms.

  • Proven ability to work independently and manage multiple audits with tight timelines.

  • Comfortable working in a dynamic, evolving environment with matrixed teams

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Proficiency in MS Excel and reporting tools.

  • Ability to work in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.

 

WHAT NEXT?

If you believe you can make a positive contribution to our team and want to be part of Liberty’s exciting future, we would love to hear from you.

Please click APPLY NOW and attach your resume.

Agencies – We’ve got this covered!

We care about your privacy and the protection of your information. Our Privacy Policy is available at https://www.libertyspecialtymarketsap.com/footer/privacy-policy/. By submitting an application for this role, you confirm that you have read, understood and consent to our Privacy Policy and further, that we may store any information you provide in the cloud and/or a third-party platform used by the Liberty Mutual Group. You also agree that we may transfer your information to another country, including a country with different privacy laws to that of your own. You further agree that we may store your information for a period of up to 5 years and that we may contact you about relevant employment opportunities with the Liberty Mutual Group.

raw_html:
Position Overview:

Our client, a growing technology company, is seeking a Business Analyst to support the development and enhancement of its platform integration and middleware solutions. This role offers the opportunity to work closely with cross-functional stakeholders, translate business requirements into technical solutions, and contribute to the delivery of innovative products within an Agile environment.

Company Benefits & Coverage:

  • Hybrid working arrangement

  • Flexi Allowance

  • 13th Month salary

  • Office near LRT

  • Season parking pass / Transport Claim

  • Annual Leaves

  • Medical Leaves / Claims

Key Responsibilities:

1. Business Analysis

  • Gather, analyze, and document business and functional requirements from internal stakeholders and external partners.

  • Translate business needs into user stories, process flows, and functional specifications for the engineering team.

  • Coordinate and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT), ensuring issues are tracked through to resolution.

2. Product Support

  • Collaborate with the Product Manager to manage and prioritize the product backlog.

  • Monitor product performance by defining and tracking key success metrics.

  • Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives.

  • Conduct market and competitor research to support product enhancement initiatives.

3. Stakeholder Engagement

  • Work closely with technical and business teams to ensure successful project delivery.

  • Prepare and maintain documentation such as PRDs, process flows, release notes, and project status reports.

  • Support partner onboarding by facilitating integration discussions and maintaining technical documentation.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor's Degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related discipline.

  • 0–2 years of experience in Business Analysis, Product Management, or a related role.

  • Strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.

  • Familiarity with API concepts (REST, JSON, Webhooks); hands-on experience is an advantage.

  • Experience using documentation and collaboration tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Google Workspace, or similar.

  • Detail-oriented with the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment.

Nice to have:

  • Exposure to platform integration, middleware, or enterprise connectivity solutions.

  • Experience working in an Agile/Scrum environment.

  • Basic understanding of API gateways, message queues, and data mapping.

  • Familiarity with tools such as Postman, Swagger/OpenAPI, or similar.

job_description: 1.   Ensure the completeness, accuracy and timely submission of regulatory data to BNM in accordance with the established timelines and guidelines.2.   Monitor and validate the completeness and accuracy of reporting details and collaborate with relevant stakeholders to ensure any discrepancies are rectified prior to submission.3.   Manage and ensure compliance with new / revised reporting requirements.4.   Perform daily reconciliations to ensure the completeness and accuracy of submissions.5.   Attend to users’ requests and provide support and guidance on matters related to CBOP reporting.6.   Assist system and product owners in conducting User Acceptance Test (UAT) for system enhancements or new product launches that may impact CBOP reporting.
raw_html:
1.   Ensure the completeness, accuracy and timely submission of regulatory data to BNM in accordance with the established timelines and guidelines.

2.   Monitor and validate the completeness and accuracy of reporting details and collaborate with relevant stakeholders to ensure any discrepancies are rectified prior to submission.

3.   Manage and ensure compliance with new / revised reporting requirements.

4.   Perform daily reconciliations to ensure the completeness and accuracy of submissions.

5.   Attend to users’ requests and provide support and guidance on matters related to CBOP reporting.

6.   Assist system and product owners in conducting User Acceptance Test (UAT) for system enhancements or new product launches that may impact CBOP reporting.

raw_html:
Hytech is a management consulting company headquartered in Australia and Singapore, focusing on providing digital transformation solutions for the fintech and Financial Services industries. Our services cover multiple areas such as back-office operation support, Human Resource Planning, payment systems, and risk control, committed to providing comprehensive and professional service support to clients.


With a team of over 2,000 professionals worldwide, we have deep industry insights and localization implementation capabilities to ensure efficient business progress while complying with local regulations. Hytech always focuses on mid- and back-end solutions, without involving any front-end or transaction-related businesses, continuously helping customers achieve secure, compliant, and sustainable digital transformation.


﻿Job Title: QC Data Analyst

Location: TW/MY


Job Responsibilities:

  • Retrieve, clean, and analyze QC-related data from databases
  • Perform cross-checking and validation of quality control data
  • Prepare analysis reports and provide actionable insights from data
  • Utilize Python and AI tools to build and improve automated QC processes
  • Support continuous improvement initiatives through data analysis


Job Requirements:

  • 2–3 years of experience in Data Analysis or Quality Control (QC)
  • Proficient in SQL and familiar with AI tools such as Claude and GPT
  • Basic to intermediate knowledge of Python for automation and data processing
  • Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
  • High sensitivity to numbers and data accuracy
  • Good problem-solving and communication skills

raw_html:
Our client is recruiting for an Intelligence Analyst to join their team in KL, Malaysia, on a 12-month contract (possible extension upon business approval)

  • Hybrid working arrangement (60/40)
  • Malaysia normal working hours (8 - 5PM or 9 - 6PM)
  • Some travel required, especially to Singapore, Jakarta and Pune - once a quarter

The Intelligence Analyst Asia Pacific is responsible for the collection, analysis and effective communication of security intelligence to support security risk management for a range of our clients' businesses in the Asia Pacific Region. The analyst will focus on tactical threats to these operating assets, working closely with our client's Asia Pacific security teams to drive down risk for the company.

About you:

  • Mid-level experienced - 3-5 years' experience
  • Experience assessing threats and/or advising on security or intelligence matters; experience in the retail and/or energy industry a plus.
  • Comfortable with handling and using data and AI to support analysis. Experience visualizing data sets desired.
  • Experience representing an organization's intelligence capabilities to internal and external stakeholders.
  • An understanding of the intelligence cycle and its application in a commercial environment.
  • Previous experience in an analytical or security environment supporting real-time business or operational activity.
  • Excellent written and analytical skills. Ability to draft detailed and accurate reports. Briefs effectively and confidently.
  • Experience with open-source intelligence tools and techniques.
  • Ability to demonstrate a high level of diligence, initiative, and accuracy with limited supervision.
  • Ability to quickly forge productive relationships and build networks

raw_html:
Objectives of the Position


  • Seek to understand business needs and provide support, services and solution on payroll, HR data, HR systems and processes.

  • Offer day-to-day support to service recipients on HR inquiries, work collaboratively with others and perform research on payroll and HR data practices by staying abreast of current and emerging market insights and legal legislations in local countries.

  • Participate in process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience.


Main Tasks


Operational Excellence


  • Ensure data quality and data integrity for Payroll and HR data processes.

  • Receive, analyze, process and follow-up on personnel administration requests via multiple request channels, ensure accurate and timely interface to the respective internal/ external processing chains i.e. payroll, finance, data analytics, external service providers, local authorities etc.

  • Manage entire termination workflow for all termination types which includes leaves calculations, information checks, letters preparation, systems updates, payment calculations and exit clearance.

  • Responsible for time and attendance administrations, including manual calculations on time allowances/deductions, maintenance of work schedules, leaves, overtimes, and shift substitutions.

  • Support the delivery of payroll inputs and administration i.e. employee subsidies, fleet management, long service awards etc.

  • Support the salary review exercises on employee data preparation, letter generations and new salary processing.

  • Responsible for the local labor administrative activities and scheduled statutory HR reporting, ensure timely and compliant deliveries as per legislation requirements.

  • Responds to recurring or ad-hoc business needs to design and to deliver country specific or customized reports which could not be auto-generated by the analytics/reporting systems.

  • Work closely with other teams and external service providers to ensure all service requests are completed accurately and timely.


Service Engagement


  • Providing advisory on employee enquiries, policy/guidelines clarifications, HR processes, HR systems guides, legislation processes etc.

  • Monitor multiples service requests to ensure services delivered by the team are in accordance with established Service Level Agreement (SLA) and internal Key Performance Indicators (KPI).

  • Perform research and information gathering on local market HR news, legislation updates and guidelines to maintain internal knowledge database.

  • Analyze, clarify, and resolve operation issues and enquiries with close collaboration with relevant Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to ensure quality services are delivered from the service hub. All unsolved and potential operational issues are escalated timely according to establish escalation process, with detailed analysis provided to Assistant Manager and Manager.


Process Improvement and Project Initiatives


  • Continuously review and update knowledge documents in areas of Payroll and People data to develop the formation of competence hub.

  • Identify opportunities, prepare and analyze business case, and participate in digitalization and process optimization. Actively work on implementation of process improvements, automation and digitalization measures which will be rolled out predominantly in the GB KL Hub for achieving the efficiency target.

  • Drive initiatives which contribute to the improvement of service experience

  • Identify deviations from the established blueprints of multiple system processors in Payroll and HR data maintenance processes and escalate to Assistant Manager and Manager in ensuring the capturing of business requirements, system settings, configuration needs and change trackers. Provide inputs and participate in user acceptance testing in system maintenance and upgrade, follow up and escalate timely with respective party to ensure fixes or changes can be delivered on time.


Team Collaboration


  • Works with moderate supervision within established procedures and practices.

  • Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously sharing knowledge with others. Assist in orientation training and act as buddy to new team member.


Job Requirements


Education


  • Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Commerce or Business Administration and Finance


Work Experience 


  • 1–2 years of experience in Payroll and HR Operations, or fresh graduates with relevant internship experience

  • Proficient in Mandarin to support China-based customers

  • Preferably experience in relevant HR procedures, practices, and country statutory

  • Preferably working knowledge of integrated payroll systems preferably on SAP.


Technical & Professional Knowledge


  • Demonstrates a good level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location.

  • Preferably understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location.

  • Strong processing skills and analytical abilities (inclination for Mathematics/Finance/Problem solving).

  • Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills.

raw_html:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

With a culture rooted in innovation, Glory’s people are transforming our customer’s businesses every day. Our automation solutions empower retailers, restaurants and financial institutions to save time, money, and deliver unrivalled customer experiences. Publicly listed and proudly international, Glory has been a trusted name for over 100 years, and a global leader for more than a decade. This reputation is built on our pioneering software and hardware combined with our dynamic people. Alongside our technology, it’s our people who drive our success. 

PURPOSE OF THE ROLE

To assist the FP&A team in delivering financial support (control, analysis and reporting) to the CFO and the wider

Executive team with focus and responsibility of specific HQ overhead functions.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Following analysis of actual results, prepare month end journals for accrual, prepayments and corrections for head office cost centres

  • Prepare and issue details of actual expenditure versus forecast and budget to central cost centre managers

  • Prepare month end analysis and commentary for overheads, including variances to budget and forecast for the International Business for inclusion in the main Executive Board reporting pack

  • Collate input and assumptions and act as finance business partner for HQ functions in the preparation of head office budgets and forecasts

  • Prepare function specific monthly reporting

  • Assist in reviewing regional performance for actual results, forecasts and budgets

  • Additional analysis and reporting tasks as required

KEY INTERFACES

  • IBHQ Cost centre owners

  • IBHQ & Regional Finance teams

  • Sales teams for transfer pricing

EDUCATIONAL QUALIFICATIONS / TRAINING

  • Ideally have experience working in a multinational finance function

  • Ideally educated to degree level

  • Ideally part/newly qualified CIMA/ACCA

REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES

  • Good analytical and data presentation skills

  • English speaking, with the ability to communicate knowledgeably and professionally with senior operatives within the business

  • Reasonable understanding of financial management, financial forecasting, budgeting and reporting

  • A team player, they will be highly self-motivated and organised, have a high attention to detail and be able to work under pressure in light of tight deadlines

  • Demonstrate ability to pro-actively suggest improvements to existing processes

  • Strong Excel skills & Power BI an advantage

  • Japanese language skills advantageous given role stakeholders

Working Hours (Monday to Friday)

Country Time Zone      Start      End
Malaysia                         11am      8pm

collaborate on data modeling and query/performance considerations.Use modern engineering practices and tools (Git, code reviews, testing, CI/CD) to deliver production-quality software.Partner with stakeholders to clarify business needs, translate them into technical solutions, and iterate based on feedback.Demonstrate ownership: break down ambiguous problems, propose options, and drive tasks to completion with minimal supervision.Required QualificationsBachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field (or equivalent experience).Graduates from top-tier universities or those holding a Master’s degree are preferred.Solid programming skills in Java and Python.Familiarity with Spring Framework (Spring Boot preferred).Understanding of microservices architecture and API development.Hands-on experience with at least one ML/DL framework, e.g. PyTorch, TensorFlow, or scikit-learnReal project experience in AI training (internship, academic, or industry), including training and evaluation of models.Working knowledge of relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL/MySQL/Oracle) and SQL.Proficiency with common development tools and workflows (Git, debugging, build tools, testing).Strong self-motivation and collaboration skills.Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have)Experience deploying AI models to production (batch/streaming/online inference) and basic MLOps concepts.Familiarity with containers and cloud (Docker/Kubernetes; AWS/Azure/GCP).Experience with distributed systems, messaging, or caching (Kafka/RabbitMQ/Redis, etc.).Ability to speak Chinese (Mandarin) is a plus.What We’re Looking For (Behavioral / Mindset)Ownership & self-directed execution: able to work independently, manage priorities, and communicate progress/risks clearly.Product mindset: understands “why” behind requirements, cares about user/business impact, and can propose improvements instead of only implementing tickets.Ideas & curiosity: brings viewpoints, experiments responsibly, and continuously learns new AI and engineering practices.Team collaboration: works effectively in a global team and contributes to a healthy engineering culture.
raw_html:
Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and maintain backend services for Sales IT systems using Java and Spring Framework/Spring Boot.

  • Build and support microservices (REST APIs, integration patterns, service reliability/observability basics).

  • Develop AI/ML-related code in Python, including data preparation, training experimentation, and evaluation.

  • Participate in AI projects with hands-on model training experience (e.g., feature engineering or embeddings, training runs, metrics, iteration, reproducibility).

  • Integrate trained models into applications (online inference APIs or batch jobs), ensuring performance, stability, and maintainability.

  • Work with relational databases and SQL; collaborate on data modeling and query/performance considerations.

  • Use modern engineering practices and tools (Git, code reviews, testing, CI/CD) to deliver production-quality software.

  • Partner with stakeholders to clarify business needs, translate them into technical solutions, and iterate based on feedback.

  • Demonstrate ownership: break down ambiguous problems, propose options, and drive tasks to completion with minimal supervision.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or a related field (or equivalent experience).

  • Graduates from top-tier universities or those holding a Master’s degree are preferred.

  • Solid programming skills in Java and Python.

  • Familiarity with Spring Framework (Spring Boot preferred).

  • Understanding of microservices architecture and API development.

  • Hands-on experience with at least one ML/DL framework, e.g. PyTorch, TensorFlow, or scikit-learn

  • Real project experience in AI training (internship, academic, or industry), including training and evaluation of models.

  • Working knowledge of relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL/MySQL/Oracle) and SQL.

  • Proficiency with common development tools and workflows (Git, debugging, build tools, testing).

  • Strong self-motivation and collaboration skills.

  • Preferred Qualifications (Nice to Have)

  • Experience deploying AI models to production (batch/streaming/online inference) and basic MLOps concepts.

  • Familiarity with containers and cloud (Docker/Kubernetes; AWS/Azure/GCP).

  • Experience with distributed systems, messaging, or caching (Kafka/RabbitMQ/Redis, etc.).

  • Ability to speak Chinese (Mandarin) is a plus.


What We’re Looking For (Behavioral / Mindset)

  • Ownership & self-directed execution: able to work independently, manage priorities, and communicate progress/risks clearly.

  • Product mindset: understands “why” behind requirements, cares about user/business impact, and can propose improvements instead of only implementing tickets.

  • Ideas & curiosity: brings viewpoints, experiments responsibly, and continuously learns new AI and engineering practices.

  • Team collaboration: works effectively in a global team and contributes to a healthy engineering culture.
